### bigknitwit — 2012-12-18T19:52:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/things-i-dont-like-about-the-fourm.4964/post-67889>

I am having a hard time getting back into reading theÂ forum regularly and staying on top of it, because I can't figure out how to do my main browsing/updating technique. Before,Â I used to simply look at the list of new posts, and go through them. I was generally always up to date, and if not, I had a backwards log of posts I'd missed. I can't seem to find that same feature on this new website, and so I'm fumbling around with the new posts window, which automatically closes after every read. This shouldn't be a big deal, but I've found my reading patterns and frequency have changed/reduced because of it, not deliberately. Is there a way to re-create this feature that I haven't found? I feel so silly that something so small is making such a big difference for me. I can't seem to get back into a reading "groove."

### Huck — 2012-12-18T20:11:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/things-i-dont-like-about-the-fourm.4964/post-67891>

> **bigknitwit said**
> I am having a hard time getting back into reading theÂ forum regularly and staying on top of it, because I can't figure out how to do my main browsing/updating technique. Before,Â I used to simply look at the list of new posts, and go through them. I was generally always up to date, and if not, I had a backwards log of posts I'd missed. I can't seem to find that same feature on this new website, and so I'm fumbling around with the new posts window, which automatically closes after every read. This shouldn't be a big deal, but I've found my reading patterns and frequency have changed/reduced because of it, not deliberately. Is there a way to re-create this feature that I haven't found? I feel so silly that something so small is making such a big difference for me. I can't seem to get back into a reading "groove."

What I do is when you 1st enter the forum, you see on the right # topics with unread posts.Â  Then I click on the down arrow to the right of it.Â  Then you have to scroll up a little, but you will see all the unread posts with an indicator telling you how many new messages are in each thread.

When you're done, go back to top and click on the icon next to the arrow (mark all topics as read).

# Thread: Employer provided food on Black Friday
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/employer-provided-food-on-black-friday.4967/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glen PDQ — 2012-11-24T14:58:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/employer-provided-food-on-black-friday.4967/post-65509>

My employer provided pizzas from Pizza Hut on Black Friday so employees wouldn't have to fight traffic going to and coming back from lunch and be late clocking back in. I went to the supermarket and brought back freshly steamed shrimp and tilapia while the others were eating the pizza. I can call ahead and have the seafood waiting for me. There is a bottle of olive oil in my locker I use for drizzling. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### maggie — 2012-11-24T16:42:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/employer-provided-food-on-black-friday.4967/post-65519>

Glen, way to think ahead! How did your co-workers react?

### Glen PDQ — 2012-11-24T18:14:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/employer-provided-food-on-black-friday.4967/post-65522>

> **maggie said**
>
> Glen, way to think ahead! How did your co-workers react?

Most of them are used to my eating habits now. I told them I would feel like crap the next day if I chowed down on pizza. Nobody responded to the statement since they were probably happy to be getting free food. The majority of them are not clued in that what they eat can affect their next two unborn generations. I mentioned that detail of epigenetics to one lady. It was a friendly atmosphere so maybe some of them were listening.

It kind of blows away the defense of those who say they can do anything they want to themselves since they aren't hurting anyone.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-29T07:38:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/employer-provided-food-on-black-friday.4967/post-65823>

totally agree.......

# Thread: How do you really start this Cold Thermogenesis thing?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-really-start-this-cold-thermogenesis-thing.4969/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### gtyner1@gmail.com — 2012-11-25T02:51:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-really-start-this-cold-thermogenesis-thing.4969/post-65533>

So I amÂ tryingÂ to change my lifestyle. Â I am way too fat and need to drop some serious poundage. Â I read alot of the articles on here and I want to really get a crack at adapting to cold. Â Right now I need a jacket in rooms that others wear shortÂ sleevesÂ shirts, so I know I have a long way to go.

Â 

On day 1 I filled a bowl full of water and put 3 trays of ice in it and let it cool down while I made my high protein / high fat breakfast. Â When I tried to stick my face in the water I startedÂ hyperventilatingÂ  Â I mean I could not hold my head down for 1 second in the water because my immediate need to breath in was over whelming.

Â 

So I kinda tilted my head so I could get my forehead under the water but my mouth was still out so I could breath. Â Now its 5 days later and the same thing happens, even though I have done this every day. Â I don't last even 1 second with my face fully immersed. Â 

Â 

Since this is the baby step of the whole process, does it get any better? Â Would I be better off taking a cold shower in the morning? Â My water isn't truely that cold yet so I wasn't sure if they would provide any real benefit.

Â 

Any advice?

Â 

Best Regards,

Â 

Geoff

### Sunny Cowgirl — 2012-11-25T08:06:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-really-start-this-cold-thermogenesis-thing.4969/post-65539>

Geoff, I'm still learning my around all this information, but I think I recall reading that you need to get yourself leptin sensitive first and then start trying to adapt to cold.

Perhaps it would help if you started with cool tap water, (without the ice) and when you can handle that better, gradually drop the temperature. Baby steps.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-25T08:29:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-really-start-this-cold-thermogenesis-thing.4969/post-65542>

http://www.jackkruse.com/easy-start-guide/

# Thread: JOY.....anyone have any lovely stories of joy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joy-anyone-have-any-lovely-stories-of-joy.4970/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2012-11-25T03:54:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joy-anyone-have-any-lovely-stories-of-joy.4970/post-65534>

Something happened today that is pretty amazing. I was at the ocean this morning playing in the water with my grandsons. It was a beautiful sunny and warm day. Yachts bobbing in the harbour. Families out for a walk. Children building sand castles. skydivers overhead ...and I had tears of joy running down my cheeks. It is amazing because I usually have tears of sadness. Today all I could see was joy and beauty everywhere and that is thanks to Dr. Kruse and all of you on this forum .....so thankyou all very much. x

### prAna303 — 2012-11-25T04:31:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joy-anyone-have-any-lovely-stories-of-joy.4970/post-65537>

I think this is what happends when we start to get rid of all the filters we have put on to ourself. Everything from fastfood, fastculture, fast whatever. When we get into our elements of nature we start to cry of joy because everything is a big part of us as species... Think how we did classical music in the past, they did have a connection to the elements.

Â 

\[video=youtube\_share;Q0DSnxHYu9Y\]http://youtu.be/Q0DSnxHYu9Y\[/video\]

### caroline — 2012-11-25T07:12:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joy-anyone-have-any-lovely-stories-of-joy.4970/post-65538>

thankyou - that is so beautiful

### Inger — 2012-11-25T08:22:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joy-anyone-have-any-lovely-stories-of-joy.4970/post-65541>

Caroline.. that was beautiful...

I cry from joy too sometimes Caroline.... And sometimes there is a mix of joy and pain I guess.. or the pain comes out in the form of tears because the joy melts the ice..? When one sees / experiences something very beautiful, like children and love, the nature, a orgasm..?

After I started to eat much raw I too felt more.. I was not so unpermeable and numb anymore? I wonder why.. I just know it does me good.

To be numb in ones heart feels not so good.. I rather take the pain, and the joy for sure!

Somehow the modern foods numbed me down..

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-25T08:55:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joy-anyone-have-any-lovely-stories-of-joy.4970/post-65545>

The more layers of illness one peels away from from your onion the more joy your brain begins to see in the life exposed around you.........the better antenna in your head the clear your mind sees its world. Â

### caroline — 2012-11-25T17:47:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joy-anyone-have-any-lovely-stories-of-joy.4970/post-65589>

Caroline.. that was beautiful...

I cry from joy too sometimes Caroline.... And sometimes there is a mix of joy and pain I guess.. or the pain comes out in the form of tears because the joy melts the ice..? When one sees / experiences something very beautiful, like children and love, the nature, a orgasm..?

After I started to eat much raw I too felt more.. I was not so unpermeable and numb anymore? I wonder why.. I just know it does me good.

To be numb in ones heart feels not so good.. I rather take the pain, and the joy for sure!

Somehow the modern foods numbed me down..

beautifully said Inger. To be numb is not good - I lived like that for quite awhile. Finally the sadness comes out and then the Joy. I definitely feel it is eating raw too - don't really understand it - but - intuitively it feels so right.
It is also children - they take so much pleasure and joy in the simplest of things. They are magic.

# Thread: Improved eyesight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-eyesight.4972/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2012-11-25T08:39:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-eyesight.4972/post-65544>

I was at the optician yesterday to check my eyesight because I do my drivers lisence right now. I was checking them last time about 8 years ago maybe? I have been without glasses all these years and wear them only when seeing movies or at school or such things. I used my old glasses maybe last time one year ago? I do not care if I see a little less. I just do not like wearing glasses at all. They are in my way. And no way would I put any contact lenses on my eyeballs, they need to breath for sure! I had -2 dioptrines on both eyes so not too bad. Now, yesterday it come out I have only -1,5 on one eye and -1,25 on the other! Yay! So I will get new glasses. Because I still want to use them when driving.. even if my doctor said I do not need to use glasses when driving but it is better for sure.

I have no idea what have helped or if I am just getting older..lol because sometimes eyesight improve when getting older too. Or it might be good nutrition and some facedunks? Or lookig in the sun in he mornings? Anyways, this made me really happy! Ok I will get some nice looking new glasses too.. at least the two women in the shop and me choosed them togehter and we agreed they looked nice.. I do look like quite a strong lady in them so I better only use them when driving..lol

### LinD — 2012-11-26T11:32:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-eyesight.4972/post-65627>

> **Inger said**
> I was at the optician yesterday to check my eyesight because I do my drivers lisence right now. I was checking them last time about 8 years ago maybe? I have been without glasses all these years and wear them only when seeing movies or at school or such things. I used my old glasses maybe last time one year ago? I do not care if I see a little less. I just do not like wearing glasses at all. They are in my way. And no way would I put any contact lenses on my eyeballs, they need to breath for sure! I had -2 dioptrines on both eyes so not too bad. Now, yesterday it come out I have only -1,5 on one eye and -1,25 on the other! Yay! So I will get new glasses. Because I still want to use them when driving.. even if my doctor said I do not need to use glasses when driving but it is better for sure.
>
> I have no idea what have helped or if I am just getting older..lol because sometimes eyesight improve when getting older too. Or it might be good nutrition and some facedunks? Or lookig in the sun in he mornings? Anyways, this made me really happy! Ok I will get some nice looking new glasses too.. at least the two women in the shop and me choosed them togehter and we agreed they looked nice.. I do look like quite a strong lady in them so I better only use them when driving..lol

Great news!Â  I love to see clearly.Â  Surely the nutrition has something to do with it.

I do sleep in Ortho-K lenses and take them out in the morning.Â  The overnight correction helps me to see clearly the whole day and fairly well the next if I don't wear them one night.Â  I like the extreme clarity so tend to regret not wearing them that one night, but I like the little break.Â  If I happen to awaken in the night, I am aware of some dryness.Â  I keep eye drops close by to use, if needed.

My DD has awful vision--- both eyes contact lens prescription is -7.Â  Really bad.........Â  She started wearing contact lenses around age 11---when she was on the dive team.Â  My word, how could she see the end of the diving board without them!Â  Eeek.Â  She is no longer diving, but still wears the lenses.

### Awainer1 — 2012-11-26T20:11:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-eyesight.4972/post-65661>

I think my eyesight might be getting better too.Â  I dont know though.Â  I know last time I went to the the eye doctor it had fiinally stopped getting worse.Â  That was just being paleo an not epipaleo/ct.Â  Things seem a little less fuzzy when I wake up in the morning but it could be my imagination.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-eyesight.4972/post-65669>

### Inger — 2012-11-27T04:04:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-eyesight.4972/post-65680>

Yes, it is nice.. and I have done no exersices or such what I know one could do to improve ones sight! If I did it might get even better? I need to start doing them because this made me just happy!

LinD.. I have a friend that has -7 too and he need his glasses with him everywhere.. That is pretty much! That is weird that you need to sleep with lenses? How can they correct when you sleep anyways and are not looking? Are they doing something to the eyes, like forming them in another shape? Because from what I have read, that is what makes one see worse... when the eyeball get formed in another shape because of the muscles around it when they get stressed and not relaxed... and that is why certain exercises should help to relax the muscles and form the eyeball in its right shape again.

Caroline, I promise I will make a picture from my new glasses on my face and put in this thread when I get them in about 2 weeks.

But promise not to laugh..ok? They are big and they are black...lol..

### prAna303 — 2012-11-27T04:59:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-eyesight.4972/post-65681>

> **Inger said**
> Because from what I have read, that is what makes one see worse... when the eyeball get formed in another shape because of the muscles around it when they get stressed and not relaxed... and that is why certain exercises should help to relax the muscles and form the eyeball in its right shape again.

Back in the days, many painters got really bad eyesight for periods of their life. I think it was their work that was to close for their eyes, got no workout for the muscles. When they did travel to the countryside they had to change perspective and watch the horizon, thatÂ´s perhaps why they got better efter a while and could continue their work later on. Today i get really sad because we give our children iPadÂ´s and smartphones and later on we wonder why they canÂ´t go thru a room without crashing into stuff AND other people.

### Inger — 2013-03-01T11:40:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-eyesight.4972/post-78482>

Okay I said I'll post a pic when I got my glasses.. so I took a pic today me wearing them, here it comes..

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I do not know but I think I look real old in them..lol I actually hate the feeling looking through lenses, the whole world feels so very different.. argh! I am going to wear them only when driving.. when I finally get my drivers lisence.
I actually do not mind seeing a little less, makes everything more relaxing for sure..lol I am so used to it as I stopped wearing glasses when 15 yo. I see less and feel more.. HA

I can see and feel this, the beautiful sunrise from my balcony.. that is about enough

Now, off to bike to the shop to look after my oysters I ordered for my weekly energy boost!

### Rachelle — 2013-03-01T13:05:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-eyesight.4972/post-78485>

lol those are in your face glasses and I like them on you. I just bought two pairs of glasses and stuck with the style I always wear--boring. Those are the style of the day and I wanted to try a pair but didn't have the nerve.

I started wearing glasses in my late 40's and I do blame the computer. We really need to constantly move our eyes and look around us all the time instead of staring into a screen. Since eating seafood and CT my eyes are getting better and I find myself taking them off more frequently. I can't watch TV without my eyes hurting so I should just get rid of it. Ugh!!! That will be hard for me. I had become oblivious to what was happening around me and while I welcome change it can be scary.

P.S. you couldn't look old even if you tried.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-01T17:05:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-eyesight.4972/post-78499>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=65681)
>
> Back in the days, many painters got really bad eyesight for periods of their life. I think it was their work that was to close for their eyes, got no workout for the muscles.

Another factor is that artists can have some lean times with poor food. I know my eyes got worse when I got an office job, but they also got better with a better diet, with no change in work.

### Inger — 2013-03-01T17:05:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-eyesight.4972/post-78500>

Yeah Rachelle.. cut the TV.. just throw it out.  That is what I did and I never regretted it. You will get so much more time for life! Life without TV is so much more cool, I promise you.
I can watch movies on my computer if I want but I have not watched one in a year or so.. I always have so much else to do, right now I am reading about quantum theory it is more interesting than any movie made I assure you..!

### shilohman — 2013-03-02T22:21:44+0200

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=65544)

> I was at the optician yesterday to check my eyesight because I do my drivers lisence right now. I was checking them last time about 8 years ago maybe? I have been without glasses all these years and wear them only when seeing movies or at school or such things. I used my old glasses maybe last time one year ago? I do not care if I see a little less. I just do not like wearing glasses at all. They are in my way. And no way would I put any contact lenses on my eyeballs, they need to breath for sure! I had -2 dioptrines on both eyes so not too bad. Now, yesterday it come out I have only -1,5 on one eye and -1,25 on the other! Yay! So I will get new glasses. Because I still want to use them when driving.. even if my doctor said I do not need to use glasses when driving but it is better for sure.
>

I do look like quite a strong lady in them so I better only use them when driving..lol

My eyesight has also improved since eating Epi-paleo. My understanding was that the improved omega 6/3 ratio in the eye increases the mitochondrial efficiency of the muscles that control our focus. Of course there may be other factors that cause an improvement.
I just wish my fine focus would come back completely so I don't need reading glasses at all.

### Pablo — 2026-01-06T20:23:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improved-eyesight.4972/post-364873>

Hello..

A friend of mine has been asking me a lot about orthokeratology (using ortho-k lenses at night to improve daytime vision). He is a policeman, so he wants to work without glasses (in case of fights) but also he is worried about daytime use of contact lenses, since I have warned him about dr- Kruse work, saying they are bad cause they block UV in the eyes and also oxygen.

What do you guys think about these Ortho-K lenses at night??? I´m also quite interested for myself, at least to use them ocasionally when I wanna to in daytime some sports that require it, like some competition sports, or some water sports (surfing, etc.)... If they were safe for human health, they would be so cool!...

I haven´t found much from Dr. Jack online abouth these Ortho-K contact lenses... Do you know what he thinks? (Or if he sees this and wants to comment would be so good...). I found this brief comment on a blog, seemingly talking positively about these contacts, or at least being less bad than regular daytime contacts:

### [TIME #4: HOW CAN MAN HELP OUR EYE CLOCK IN A TECH WORLD?](https://jackkruse.com/how-man-helps-the-eye-clock/)

BLOG TAKE HOME 1: WHEN WE COVER OUR PUPIL WILL ALTER THE LIGHT IT SENSES???? WILL THIS SPEED UP TIME SO THAT WE CAN GET SICK AND DIE EARLIER? BLOG TAKE HOME 2: Hopes are like Purple light. Fears are like blue light. Now stop and ponder that and then ruminate on this:

 jackkruse.com

**"_CONTACTS OR GLASSES?_**
_
There are two primary issues, oxygen transmission and UV transmission that we need to concern ourselves with. The contact industry has always worked towards increasing O2 transmission by mainly by adding silicone to the lens. The O2 transmission has a massive effect photoelectrically because of the electronegativity of oxygen. This can create some comfort issues. Some people cannot wear silicone lenses. Wearing glasses, of course, fix this issue. Y**ou could consider orthokeratology where you wear a lens at night and your eye breaths normally all waking hours. This is done with a rigid gas permeable lens with a very high DK (O2 transmission/permeability)**_".

What do you think guys??? Otho-K contact lenses could be a decent option, or are they unsafe/unhealhy long term? Could they be ok for ocasional use at night, for doing some kind of sports the next day with good vision, or risky thing??

# Thread: The 8th most important thing you need to know about Diet and Exercise
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-8th-most-important-thing-you-need-to-know-about-diet-and-exercise.4973/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barry — 2012-11-25T14:34:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-8th-most-important-thing-you-need-to-know-about-diet-and-exercise.4973/post-65569>

Nothing revolutionary here... just an add-on to some of my previous posts. This is a piece I wrote for a national newspaper... so it's put in more "laymans" terms... which may seem a bit basic for some on this forum... still, hopefully some reminders and a rehash of what I've already written about... but in Muhammad Ali words:

"It's the repetition of affirmations that leads to belief, and once that belief becomes a deep conviction, things begin to happen."

**Â 

The whole is only as good as the sum of its parts**

Â 

Improving your health and fitness is a multifactorial equation. It involves many different factors which all have to be figured out and then put in place. Once they are in place, they need to be put in the right order so that they learn how to work in harmony. The equation and the factors are different for everyone. This is a crucial point to understand. There is no one equation that we can all use. Everything needs to be put in context and the context has to be based on the individual.

Having said that, there are certain principles that can be applied in order to put that equation together. These principles need to be correct and valid. If you start off with the wrong principles then you are destined for failure. Nutrition is one of these principles. It is part of the equation, but I will emphasize the word â€œpartâ€.

You see, modern nutrition and health is based on magic bullets and quick fixes. If you want to lose weight, you are told to go on a magic two week cabbage soup diet. If you want to lower your cholesterol, you are told to drink a yoghurt drink with magical plant extracts. Apart from the fact the neither of these things solve the problem, they are both just easy fixes. Easy fixes donâ€™t work. Unfortunately, when it comes to health, the majority of people are always looking for the quick way out. Take a multivitamin, to get all your vitamins. Drink a smoothie to get all your fruits. Take a special powder to get all your antioxidants. This is not what proper nutrition is about and itâ€™s not the approach you should take if you want to improve your health.

As the title of this article suggests, nutrition is about the sum of its parts. Itâ€™s not about just taking a multivitamin and a few fish oil capsules and itâ€™s not about simply eliminating one certain bad food or adding one certain type of good food back in. In order to truly get the benefits of proper nutrition, you need to make wholesale changes to the way you eat. These changes need to be based on the most modern concepts, the best (non biased/commercially funded) science, and fit with your individual make up.

I have written 24 articles so far for this magazine (Fit Mag). I only have a few hundred words to explain the millions of words of theory I have read and studied. I am essentially only scratching the surface but I am trying to get the key points across. The key points are many. Iâ€™ve talked about how the foods we should eat are not those that are falsely advertised to us as being healthy. Iâ€™ve talked about carbohydrate and its affect on insulin. Iâ€™ve talked about protein and its importance for our immune and endocrine system. Iâ€™ve talked about particular foods which can help certain conditions like sleep and stress. Iâ€™ve talked about how to recover from training and Iâ€™ve talked about how fat metabolism works and how to fat adapt. In all these articles, Iâ€™ve talked about general foods and how important the quality and source of that food you eat is. There is a difference between eating grass fed pasture raised animal meats to those that are grain fed and factory farmed. There is a difference between eating real butter and refined vegetable oil butters. So there are a lot of things to take into account. But the key point is that in order for the equation to work, you need to cover all the bases.

The problem is that people generally only want to cover the easy bases and cherry pick. I get many emails and comments from people that they are now happily eating loads of bacon and eggs with lashings of butter. Yet they are still not losing the weight they want to lose and still donâ€™t feel well. This is how the cherry picking causes problems. People generally only choose to do things that they like when it comes to food. When I delve a bit further, these are the type of people that still have 3 slices of toast with their â€œlashingsâ€ of butter and they choose to buy meats which have added sugars and refined oils. The types of fat you eat and the amount of fat you eat is completely dependent on whether or not you have covered all the other bases. The other bases are related to reducing your carbohydrate intake, eliminating gluten, getting your timing right, reducing inflammation, avoiding any added sugars, eating the right fats, eating the right proteins, and getting the best sources of these foods (to name but a few). Therefore, if you think simply eating more butter and eggs is the solution to all your problems, and then you are completely missing the point. If you still eat the foods that are causing the problems and still have the metabolic impairment (i.e. you have spent your life eating metabolically damaging foods like refined grains, oils and sugars) , then simply adding more eggs and butter to your general diet is not going to do much, in fact, it is likely to cause more problems.

My aim here is not to cause confusion and fear. It might sound like I am trying to make nutrition sound complicated. Iâ€™m not. I am simply trying to emphasize the importance of taking a collective approach to the way you eat. Itâ€™s not about making one or two changes and just adding the foods you like back into your diet while keeping all the bad stuff there. Thatâ€™s equivalent to putting in a few traffic wardens in to a completely grid locked city of traffic jams. The solution to this is to build new roads and bridges. Itâ€™s the same with your health. Address the cause instead of just patching up the symptoms. Â 

The right nutrition for improved health and fitness is about making wholesale changes to what, when and how much you eat. Itâ€™s about creating a new normal. Itâ€™s about educating yourself and learning about all the facets of good nutrition and then applying it to yourself.Â  Look to improve all aspects of the food you eat and the way you eat. How detailed you want that to be is down to the person. And like I said before, everything has to be put into context and individualised. The right guidelines are there if you look in the right places, the right foods are easily available and Ireland happens to be blessed with really good quality sources. The whole is only as good as the sum of its parts. Learn what those parts are and gather as many of them as you can. Make it a long term continuous project so that it becomes habitual. Health and wellness should not be a temporary state, so why should eating healthily be something you do temporarily??

Â 

Furthermore, remember that there are still several other factors of the equation that need to be put in place like good sleep, reduced stress and proper exercise. Each of these things is a separate discussion but itâ€™s important to mention them. Health is a multifactorial equation and nothing works in isolation. So you might have the best nutrition in the world and eat the â€œBarryâ€ way. Great. But if you suffer from stress and anxiety and sleep poorly, then you are likely to still suffer from health problems. Â 

So the best advice I have for anyone trying to improve their health is this: do not look for magic bullets, they don't exist. Rather, look to improve every aspect of your life from nutrition to thoughts to exercise no matter how big or small they may seem. Everything counts. Nothing is more important than the other. You are only as good as the sum of your parts.

Â 

"Beyond the walls of intelligence, life is defined"

### freesia — 2012-11-29T21:58:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-a-glass-of-wine-after-ct-most-likely-ok.4979/post-65907>

Updating a few days late...and not because I've been conked out somewhere in a drunken stupor!

Glass of wine was fine. Zero ill effects.

...None the next night when I had one either. SecondÂ night theÂ wineÂ was about anÂ hour and a half after my bath.

**WHY DO I DARE TO DISAGREE with Paleo and modern healthcare, like the thought of Obamacare?

If our goal is to really understand our world better, our**Â lack of scientific diversity**Â is what really makes it a more difficult task. Â I am no exercise guru, gym owner, or research scientist. Â But they are no experts on neural biology. Â And humans have big brains, and this is what separates us from all eutherian mammals. Â I am a clinician who thinks best outside the box and lets the science guide me. Â Paleo can be described as a team or a tribe today. Â Team building requires you to limit diversity of thought, and subjugate it for a common goal. Â It sounds counterintuitive at first, Â but this is what the concept of â€œ[group think](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groupthink)â€ is all about. Â There is an entire social science built upon this subject and concept. Â When people begin to all share values and scientific beliefs, they become a sort of â€œteamâ€. Moreover, once you engage the â€œscience of teamâ€, it shuts down open minded thinking that goes against what the tribe believes to be true. Â Normally this is not a big issue, if things are truly correct scientifically, for those values or beliefs the group holds sacred.

_But what if they are not correct?_**Â  In my view, that is my role on the team. Â Someone has to challenge conventional thought even in an unconventional group of people. Â I am that guy. Â It makes me a target. Â I am OK with it, because I am looking out for our optimal health, and nothing else. Â This path got my own health back. Â I think it is worth sharing with you too.

What if you are not aware of something you never considered? Â  What my blog represents, is that guy, who dares to disagree with the current beliefs. Â I tell all my members and readers to question everything, including what I tell them, or write about. Â I already know I donâ€™t know everything, even though many of my critics will put words in my mouth that suits their agenda best.

One thing I do know about, is how this information help fix me and my family. Â It then helped me fix many patients. Â We did not get the results found on most paleo sites doing it their way. Â We used a different method. Â Because we did it differently does that mean they were wrong and we were right? Â No. Â It means we have more to learn about each method. Â When you can not get the results you see from the public testamonials we are all accustomed to seeing weekly, I want you to know why you canâ€™t. Â I do not want you to be depressed or shell shocked because the advice given elsewhere just does not work for you. Â I do not want you thinking you are broken deeply somehow. Â I rather enjoy a collaborative model of scientific exploration, that requires my thinking brothers and sisters, not to become echo chambers of the same beliefs the tribe currently has.**

In order to get different results we need to think differently about things at times. Â The science of DHA alone, tells you something is very off in the current belief structure. Â I want to beÂ _**that lone nut**_**, that gets you to look at something the whole tribe believes to be true for yourself. Â  But do it without having to worry about what the tribe thinks of you or your methods. Â That is what the comment section of my blog is for. Â Itâ€™s for you to exert your ability to think differently than you usually might given the tribeâ€™s beliefs. Â I want everyone who is sick and tired of being sick and tired to come here and read. Â I want the weary who have failed the Rx found in the medical books we have all read to come find out why. Â My blog is not for those of you who have found success easily. Â **It is for the people who have not found success for a reason.Â Â I want you to know why everyoneâ€™s mileage varies in this journey to optimal. Â I do not want you thinking there is something wrong with you. Â I want to teach how our â€˜Ferrariâ€™ really works, and for you to discover that you too can reclaim your health when you understand the owners manual you came with. Â I agree with most of what they say, but on brain biochemistry they do not have it all together. Â That is their puzzle. Â As a clinician my job is to make sense of our present biochemistry puzzle. Â Everyone of us is at a different point on the same continuum of human biochemistry. Â Deciphering where you are at now is my domain. Â There is a scientific epistemologic basis of how the laws of biochemistry apply to our species, and that is universal for our species. I am trying to show you how it works in an evolutionary context, and not how we think it works.

The concept of â€œteamâ€ requires confirmation in the face of adversity. Â I want to you embrace that discomfort with this information, and realize that all of what we believe may not be what we all thought. Â Will, you allow yourself to examine things for yourself andÂ dare to disagree**Â with your perceptions of your current truth?

Victory is had by opening eyes on both sides. Thereâ€™s room for many opinions, as long as we all warn the readers that with or without degrees, they have to take their own lives in their own hands**

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-29T16:37:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-prevention-pushed-by-doctors-female-breast-removal-before-age-35.4981/post-65868>

> **Jack Kruse said**
> When you limit options due to cost you are forcing........and this is surely happening daily in hospitals across america whether Darleen believes it or not. Â What it will foster is a second tier system for people who reject Darleen's beliefs ( not meant to piss ya off Darleen, but used to illustrate a point) for what they want to create in their own life.......and that choice will require a lot more wisdom smarts and money. Â Healthcare will be available to all but good care will become quite rare for the illness at hand. Â Evidence based medicine is medicine that gives the best smallest improvement for the lowest cost possible........regardless of the longterm outcome. Â the short term outcome is now how medicine will be practiced.......and most who advocate for this system do not understand the implications of this algorithm.........I do because I play in this sand box daily.

The issue has been for over 30 years health care COSTS which went completely out of control in the 1980's AFTER deregulation. It allowed FOR-PROFITS to buy up hospitals, doctor's private practices and insurance companies. Â It has become a system run by racketeers. Â It's no better than the mob.

Â 

When I was in my 20's we had the best health care in the world and the it was affordable for all. Â Business were happy to offer insurance coverage as an incentive to hire the best employees because it was affordable.

Â 

I have stated this all before and will now do so again. Â Just to make my point. At 22 I had an appendectomy. Â I was working for Retail Credit Corp as a secretary/investigator and they paid my health insurance premiums in full. Â When I came out of the hospital I didn't owe ANYone a dime. Â My insurance covered it all.

Â 

At 27 I tore the cartilage in my right knee. Â I was working on the assembly line at Teledyne Aqua Tec and as part of my wages they paid my insurance premiums. Â When I limped out of the hospital after having the cartilage removed I owed NOTHING.

Â 

then the 80's and deregulation hit. Â My right knee collapsed and it was grinding bone on bone. Â More surgery. Â THIS time the insurance company told me "too bad so sad" we're only going to ALLOW half your bill of which we will pay 80% and you can pay the other half PLUS 20% of the remainder. Now mind you, by this time DH was working for the BNRR and we had supposedly the best coverage OUR money could buy. Â That's right. Â Now it was coming out of our pocket as part of the wages contract. Â And it was well over $500/month (remember this was 30 years ago).

Â 

Then I had to have my gall bladder removed. Â I had to go to Billings to have it done because the local hospital didn't yet have the equipment to do the "laser" method which is the least invasive. Â AGAIN the blankety blank blank insurance company DISALLOWED a HUGE portion of the bill and then only paid 80% of the lesser amount. Â When I filed a complaint they said too bad so sad ask your surgeon the cut his bill. Â Now seriously Jack ... if you operated on someone and they then came back and said the insurance company said you charged too much would you lower your bill? Â didn't think so.

Â 

Ditto with my hysterectomy. Â Same song, same verse, different day.

Â 

The CEO of that insurance company, btw, retired with a near BILLION DOLLAR PAYDAY. Â Where'd the money come from? Â People like me who were denied reimbursement "just because" so the company could grow fat. Â Just like a blood sucking tick.

Â 

I hate insurance companies and what they have done. Â I hate the fact that Congress wrote this stupid assed bill (The Affordable Health Care Act) when they could have done one of two things to permanently fix this mess. Â They could have put the regulations back in place that kept the greed mongers at bay OR they could have opened Medicare to everyone. Â Single payer or regs. Â Either way would fix the problem.

Â 

that's MY opinion and until someone can show me otherwise I'm stickin' to it.

Yes, I think socialized medicine is possibly better than letting people die. That's the case here in Spain. Nevertheless I think very different now of the medical establishment than two years ago. There is no effort to try to explore the causes of disease in order to prevent it. I am certainly guilty of having been gullible, thinking that the dietary advice was based on science. Certainly I contributed to rot the traditional diet of my family: margarine, low fat milk and everything else. I think that had quite a weight on the early demise of my mother (cancer) and my grandmother (heart attack). So I don't delegate our health in them now.

Â 

Going back to my point. Appendicitis is a western diet disease: [see minute 20 of the video posted by Dr. Kruse](http:///forum-2/educating-doctors/see-how-a-paleo-doc-has-to-navigate-the-waters-of-his-colleagues-to-get-the-message-thru/). As is commented on the iodine thread, fibroids and uterus problems may be due to iodine deficiency. Certainly orthopedic medicine will be still needed, but once all of the preventable disease are prevented the lighter burden on the system would do it affordable, both socialized or not.

And yes, I agree on [the GREED problem](http://http://www.honestmedicine.com/2009/03/burt-berkson-md-phd-talks-with-honest-medicine-about-his-work-and-our-medical-system-the-interview-t.html).

**
But Jack, you are once again missing my point. Â so let me back the truck up and take another run at it.

Â 

Insurance was initially formed to protect the insured. Â The way it is SUPPOSED to work is this. Â Let's use fire insurance as an example. Â 100 families form an insurance coop. Â They each pay in a set amount each month, let's make the arithmetic easy ... $10 per family. Â so their little coop is socking away $1000 a month or $12,000 a year. Â 5 years in, Farmer Jone's barn burns down. Â the insurance cooperative pays to put up Â a new barn.

All goes well until the coop account starts to get too big. Â Carl Carpetbagger gets wind of it and offers to "manage' their fund for a small fee. Â Pretty soon CC needs a bigger office and a secretary and a salesman and a new car and a bigger salary and pretty soon the expenses he's running up outstrip the money he feels he can pay out to the insureds. Â So now we're going to start putting conditions on how much an insured can have back of _THEIR MONEY._**

Â 

It's got nothing to do with where the farmers are living and everything to do with what has now become a protection racket because Carl Carpetbagger now has the Congress Critters in his back pocket and he sees to it (by writing the legislation) that everyone HAS TO BUY INSURANCE. Â Protection racket.

Why should ANYone have to live in a big city just so they can get decent health care at a reasonable cost? Â There are a LOT of doctors living here because this is a great area. Â the local hospital has become a PROFIT center which is BS in my book. Â they are not there to make a profit but to care for the sick. Â At least that's how it used to be. Â I am, unfortunately, old enough to remember how things used to work and they worked VERY WELL.

I will repeat ... insurance should NOT BE FOR PROFIT. Â It is money pooled by the INSUREDS to help defray costs in emergencies. Â for a CEO to STEAL a billion dollars from an insurance company as his "just compensation" while denying the insureds who put the money there to begin with!! is a crime that just takes my breath away.

I do NOT get why no one else seems to get this.

As to the OP ... it does not help that "celebrities" are now lopping off their breasts because they have the "cancer gene and OMG get rid of them quick!" Â People are not only sheep but they refuse to think for themselves. Â And then there is big pharma and (sorry Jack) the AMA not to mention the insurance companies and FOR PROFIT hospitals ... Once again I say FOLLOW THE (expletive deleted) MONEY. Â 

Â 

ETA, Jack I never said the surgeon was responsible. Â I was absolutely AGHAST that the insurance company would try to dump their share of the bill by telling ME to ask the surgeon to lower his bill. Â I REFUSED. Â You know why? Â Because the surgeon had to go learn new techniques so why should I ask him to cut his bill? I would not! Â It wasn't that he charged more than other places either. Â The insurance company had a responsibility to PAY THEIR FAIR SHARE. Â I was NOT the only person who went through this. Â It was NOT a matter of know what was in my policy. Â The point was they simply, as a matter of policy, DENIED large chunks of legitimate claims because they wanted to keep the money. Â Remember I have said it twice now ... the ceo of that insurance company retired a few years ago with a BILLION DOLLAR PAYDAY. Â Where do you think that money came from?? Â It came from the pockets of the insured and from denying every single claim that came across the claims desk.

# Thread: Bone Broth temp question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-temp-question.4982/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### raikenreiner@gmail.c — 2012-11-26T13:36:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-temp-question.4982/post-65634>

The bone broth I'm making is around 190s...is this alright?Â  The slow cooker descriptions I've seen all say to set it on low....but is meant to be low or the lowest setting on the slow cooker?Â  I'm thinking some are different than others and the directions never mention other settings.

Â 

I read that you can start with a boil...but than go down to a simmer on the stove or set it on low for a slow cooker with no mention of other settings.

My slow cooker has High, Low, and Warm which my mom said is the lowest setting.Â  Should I keep it on Low....or Warm?Â  I switched it to warm and it hasn't seemed to go down in temp but it did have bubbles and now it doesn't so...it seems to have gone down with the visual but my thermometer isn't saying its lower....weird

Â 

I was wondering if I should use the High temperature for a set amount of time and than switch to low or warm as well?Â  I started with high for about an hour and a half.Â  Would it be ok to start with warm or low?Â  I've heard its better to boil some first...

Â 

Thanks

### diane — 2012-11-26T13:45:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-temp-question.4982/post-65637>

I put my slow cooker on "low" for at least 24 hours. I don't put it high first - just start it right out from low and leave it there. To me, warm is after something is cooked.

Â 

I know that many people on here cook it for 48 - 76 hours on low - so you can definitely go longer than 24 hours.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-11-26T16:19:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-temp-question.4982/post-65647>

I start mine out on high to get it to a boil and then turn it down to low and let it cook for about 2 days.Â  I have noticed my slow cooker seems to run a little hotter than I think it should be but "warm" is way too warm.Â  I'm making some right now! We are drinking this twice a day now and loving it.

### raikenreiner@gmail.c — 2012-11-26T17:15:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-temp-question.4982/post-65652>

Anyone with experience using Feedlot bones...conventional raised stuff?Â  I read something that mentioned it being better than nothing at all...everyone agree?

### furface — 2012-12-10T18:51:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-temp-question.4982/post-67049>

> **raikenreiner@gmail.c said**
> Anyone with experience using Feedlot bones...conventional raised stuff?Â  I read something that mentioned it being better than nothing at all...everyone agree?

If a person is going to slow cook it for at least 24 hours, it would probably kill any impurities the conventional raised beef, lamb, pork, or whatever may have in it anyway. Esp since grass-fed is so hard to find, and soo much more expensive...esp if it's just for bone broth. Some may feel other wise, but this is JMHO.

### kathylu — 2012-12-10T22:19:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-temp-question.4982/post-67081>

I try to use GF bones...but if I don't have any, then conventional it is! Remember to add the vinegar...very important to solubilize the minerals. I use 2 tsp ACV per quart of water.

### furface — 2012-12-10T22:57:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-temp-question.4982/post-67085>

> I try to use GF bones...but if I don't have any, then conventional it is! Remember to add the vinegar...very important to solubilize the minerals. I use 2 tsp ACV per quart of water.

I never knew that about the vinegar. Thanks, Kathy.

### kathylu — 2012-12-11T16:04:54+0200

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth-temp-question.4982/post-67152>

I never allow my bone broth to come to a hard boil. Also, I cook mine much less hours than most do. Chicken broth - 4 hrs, and beef about 5 - 6 hours. Mine comes great and it gels.

# Thread: CTing in the shower, which BAT to focus spray on?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cting-in-the-shower-which-bat-to-focus-spray-on.4987/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ljbprrfmof — 2012-11-27T18:36:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cting-in-the-shower-which-bat-to-focus-spray-on.4987/post-65723>

I have trouble getting in and out of my tub. It is perfect for CTing. Due to my stroke two years ago, I have trouble lifting my legs over the side to both get in and out.

So I continue to shower my CT. At my old house, the shower had a fixed shower head as well as a hand held. I was able to have spray from both because it was worn. Where I live now, I have both, but it is one or the other.

My question is should I let the water hit my back or chest? Is the BAT around the spine bigger than that around the sternum?

When I use the hand held, I can move it around, but I grow weary of this and actually can sit under the fixed shower head for longer periods of time.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-28T09:10:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cting-in-the-shower-which-bat-to-focus-spray-on.4987/post-65762>

Cold water on neck (carotid arteries) and upper back and chest is the best choice with this method

### ljbprrfmof — 2012-11-29T10:32:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cting-in-the-shower-which-bat-to-focus-spray-on.4987/post-65828>

Thank you Dr. Kruse.

I have found that sitting on my shower chair with the shower focused on my shoulders is easy to do.

Since I moved the chair in the shower to make this easier, I have found that I can spend more time in the shower and consequently get the water colder.

Now it will be perfect once I get to hear the radio or music as I CT.

# Thread: Jack Missed This About Leptin Rx.........
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-missed-this-about-leptin-rx.4992/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thor — 2012-11-28T02:01:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-missed-this-about-leptin-rx.4992/post-65749>

.

you can tellÂ me I'mÂ wrong go ahead but....Â  roughly 60% of the US population has atleast one genetic error on MTHFR C677T or 1298C.

Â 

Only two doctors in the entire US have a clue about the real problem this has on your health - Dr Ben Lynch out of Seattle and Dr Kendal Stewart out of Houston.Â  More than 70 disorders have the main cause rooted in MTHFR snp.Â  see [www.mthfr.net](http:///www.mthfr.net")Â 

Â 

Here's where things really get interesting.... We know the impact of Choline (B4)Â has with insulin resistance & leptin resistance plus fatty liver issues.Â  Choline main role is in what's called theÂ **Dawn Phenomenon (_similar disorder:Â Somogyi Effect).Â Â  And, choline has dozens of other areas it impacts ranging from fertility to immunity.

Â 

If you have the genetic hickupÂ MTHFR C677TÂ and/orÂ 1298CÂ - guess what.... you do not have any Choline in your system!Â  Due to how MTHFR works it "sucks" up any Choline you may have taken in from food.Â  With zero Choline available you're in HUGE trouble - that leads to Leptin and Insulin resistance on the fact track!Â 

Â 

Here's a brief geek breakdown:_**_

Choline and folate are interrelated in 1-carbon metabolism, mostly because of their shared function as methyl donors for homocysteine remethylation. Folate deficiency and mutations of methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase (MTHFR) reduce the availability of a major methyl donor, 5-methyltetrahydrofolate, which in turn lead to compensatory changes in choline metabolism.

Choline generally refers to the various [quaternary ammonium salts](http:///forum-2/leptin-rx/"/wiki/Quaternary_ammonium_salts") containing the _N,N_,N_\-trimethylethanolammonium [cation](http:///forum-2/leptin-rx/"/wiki/Cation")

Â 

[http://www.annualreviews.org/doi/abs/10.1146/annurev.nutr.26.061505.111156](http:///www.annualreviews.org/doi/abs/10.1146/annurev.nutr.26.061505.111156")

Â 

Bottom line - get tested for MTFHR snp.Â  If you have the genetic hickup you'll need a larger dose of Choline than the average person. In addition you'll need a Doctor who can properly Tx the MTHFR defect.Â  You can never heal properly until the MTHFR issues is addressed.

Â 

I know first hand the hell this disorder causes - I nearly died from this!Â  /wp-includes/images/smilies/icon\_sad.gif Â Â Â Â  I'd love to chat with Jack about this sometime but I know he's super busy.Â 

Â 

/forum-2/leptin-rx/"/wp-content/sp-resources/forum-image-uploads/thor/2012/11/404461\_367700289925477\_999377141\_n.jpg"

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-28T18:18:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/immortality-via-a-jellyfish.4995/post-65793>

> After more than 4,000 years â€” almost since the dawn of recorded time, when Utnapishtim told Gilgamesh that the secret to immortality lay in a coral found on the ocean floor â€” man finally discovered eternal life in 1988. He found it, in fact, on the ocean floor. The discovery was made unwittingly by Christian Sommer, a German marine-biology student in his early 20s. He was spending the summer in Rapallo, a small city on the Italian Riviera, where exactly one century earlier Friedrich Nietzsche conceived â€œThus Spoke Zarathustraâ€￾: â€œEverything goes, everything comes back; eternally rolls the wheel of being. Everything dies, everything blossoms again. . . .â€￾
>
>
>
> Sommer was conducting research on hydrozoans, small invertebrates that, depending on their stage in the life cycle, resemble either a jellyfish or a soft coral. Every morning, Sommer went snorkeling in the turquoise water off the cliffs of Portofino. He scanned the ocean floor for hydrozoans, gathering them with plankton nets. Among the hundreds of organisms he collected was a tiny, relatively obscure species known to biologists as Turritopsis dohrnii. Today it is more commonly known as the immortal jellyfish. Â 

Rest of article here: Â http://www.nytimes.com/2012/12/02/magazine/can-a-jellyfish-unlock-the-secret-of-immortality.html?hp&\_r=0

# Thread: High Fasting Insulin Level Question - Hack My Labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-fasting-insulin-level-question-hack-my-labs.4996/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-fasting-insulin-level-question-hack-my-labs.4996/post-65808>

.

I'm perplexed by my labs that just came back.Â Â 

Â 

All hormones are within normal range - thyroid, rT3, Test, DHEA, IGF ect...Â Â  No worries there.

Leptin level came back 9.1 on a range of 3-15 so worries there - **NOT leptin resistantÂ  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**

FBG was 100

Fasting Insulin level was rather high @ 22

Vit D was 51

### KiwiLauren — 2012-11-29T20:01:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-fasting-insulin-level-question-hack-my-labs.4996/post-65891>

Your trigs are elevated, you have inflammation (hsCRP), your cortisol is low across the day, your E is high (wondering if your T is low), I'm guessing your melatonin is also low (though I didn't see a lab) and your DHEA is low. Â So it looks like hormone imbalances, cortisol-melatonin axis issues and overall inflammation. Â As Gretchen said, JanSz can hack a lab better than most, but those results would seem to suggest both some sort of leptin resistance and other systemic issues.

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-30T11:35:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-fasting-insulin-level-question-hack-my-labs.4996/post-65958>

when did you have this set of labs? Â  What types of foods were you eating?Â 

KiwiLauren hit the big ones.Â 

Â 

Here's what I'm seeing:

your APO B:A ratio is high. your Trigs are way too high and indicates your eating grains - you want your Trigs under 50 if you're eating paleo, your HDL is Low - which is also an indication of eating grains and excess carbs out of season. you vitamin D is low you want it above 70. Â your HS Crp is high, as is your homocyctine - you've got serious inflammation. Â 

your TSH looks high - but since there's no ranges can't be for sure. Â your T4 is low, you T3 is ok but isn't in the top Quartile and your free T3 looks good - you might have a Thyroid issue. Â Additionally a high TSH coupled w/ LH/FSH issues can be an indication of pitutary problems in men - I'm not well versed in reading LH/FSH in men - Quelsen is your guy for that... PM him.... and he'll take a look when he can.Â 

your Testosterone, looks good, you're bumping into the Â low end of the top quartile - but you're going to want to keep an eye on it. b/c your DHEA is low, as is your IGF-1. I'd bet your IL-6 and TNF are elevated. Â your Estradiol is high and that's something to be concerned about.Â 

your Brain Gut axis is off. Â you need to go back re-read the BG series be 100% compliant eating the Leptin Rx with an Epi-Paleo template that incorporates the EPCOTRx. Â You also need to get your butt in some cold baths - this coupled with the Leptin Rx and eating Epi Paleo with a focus on ketosis will bring down your inflammation as well as your FBG/Fasting Insulin and HbA1C. Â Additionally b/c you're HDL is below 60 as a man - you want to ditch all nightshades until the HDL comes up - so you need to ditch any peppers/tomatoes untill that happens.Â

# Thread: Loss of Ask Jack

### ljbprrfmof — 2012-11-29T10:25:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loss-of-ask-jack.4998/post-65826>

I particularly morn the loss of Ask Jack. Since my last visit to the thread before the conversion, there have been several threads started that pertain to me. I would love to read them.

It is extremely frustrating to be able to read the topics and not the actual threads.

In the past, I was able to afford the $48/month. Now I struggle to keep my family together and fed. That is a considerable sum to be able to read questions and answers. Some answers were merely directional signs to where to find the answer. A nearly $600/yr for the privilege to view the thread and maybe get an easy answer if you post a question.

When my business was open, I found that as I explained the dynamics of my product, I sold more. Because, I found with the better knowledge of how my product wore out and why, my customers did better, even if it was with one of my competitors.

May I suggest letting the thread be read, And as an enticement to be able to post a question in the thread, a much more moderate price for those of us who struggling in this economy. That way you can stair step people up to the more expensive memberships.

Thank you

John

### SueC — 2012-11-29T15:28:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loss-of-ask-jack.4998/post-65859>

I, too, lament the loss of Ask Jack, especially because so often questions by others would clarify points that I found vague in the blogs. I also cannot afford to upgrade at this time as my hubby was recently part of a massive layoff (102 of a total staff of 149!). Yet I'm really grateful for all the incredible information here that's available for free, including the months I got to read Ask Jack. It still astounds me.

I also wonder how on earth Jack could answer the many questions that appeared in Ask Jack along with all the other things he does to live his life. I don't know how the decision came about, but it seems reasonable to me to put a price tag on the thread simply to limit a little one of the many things he has to do in any given day...

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-29T20:10:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loss-of-ask-jack.4998/post-65896>

Â I think I answer most questions on the blog........ask Jack is for the members who want and value my time. Â If this is not acceptable, please accept my apologies. It was my decision and no one else's. Â I try to give 150% of my abilities when I can. Â

### ljbprrfmof — 2012-11-29T20:54:59+0200

And in truth you responded to my question on CTing when I was hoping to get a reply from AKman. It was his thread in MDA that turned me on to the process.

I look forward to bumping into you in the other threads as I bump along to optimal health and optimal earnings.

# Thread: Hack My Adrenal Fatigue Symptoms
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-adrenal-fatigue-symptoms.5000/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TheKid — 2012-11-29T12:00:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-adrenal-fatigue-symptoms.5000/post-65834>

I am hoping someone can help me sift through some symptoms.

Basically, I am trying to determine whether what I am experiencing is due to adrenal fatigue, mitochondrial disease, or both.

My problems starting back in 2009 when I began to get what I believe were panic attacks. I would have a pounding heartbeat, chest discomfort and sometimes my heart would race suddenly. I would go to the ER only to have docs tell me everything was OK.

These seemed to resolve, but my health has never really improved. I am afraid that instead of actually resolving, I simply moved on to the next stage of adrenal fatigue.

In late summer I did a 4x salivary cortisol that came up 12, 0, 0, 0. Jack was extremely concerned about the goose eggs I put up. But I don't know what stage of adrenal fatigue that puts me at.

These days, I am all over the map. When I try to exercise, I get knocked on my backside for sometimes 2 weeks. THe fatigue is crushing.

But what worries me more than anything, is chest pain and shortness of breath. Two weeks ago, after doing some high intensity interval training, got really serious chest pain. It honestly felt like I had a rock in my chest instead of a heart. Of course I rushed to the cardiologist, and after a EKG and echo, I was once again told I was NOT having a heart attack.

That rock hard feeling is gone, but my days are up and down and literally all over the place. Chest pain is frequent, as is mood. Sleep is erratic. And I have these terrible adrenaline rushes. The rushes used to come once a night, but then increased to twice a night and even crept up during the day. I also am nearly always short of breath with any kind of physical activity, even walking. Since the cardio told me I surely don't have congestive heart failure, I am at a loss.

One thing Jack has me looking into is mitochondrial dysfunction and disease. But with all this overlapping symptomology, I don't know which it would be.

My endo wants to start me on hydrocortisone and DHEA. I am also planning to start some D-Ribose as well.

Last 5 days, I have basically been unable to leave the house. So I'd appreciate any words of wisdom from folks who've already navigated this territory.

My main concern is that hydrocortisone is a damn powerful hormone, and notoriously hard to come wean off; and that's IF your adrenals kick back in. I am thinking to hit the D-Ribose first to see what it's effect would be. Any help would be appreciated. Thanks.

### Birdy — 2012-11-29T12:24:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-adrenal-fatigue-symptoms.5000/post-65837>

Sorry I can't help you more.Â  I'd say if anyone even thinks that they have adrenal fatigue, they shouldn't be exercising.Â  Lucky for me I was always borderline AF, but the best thing I did for myself is to quit any kind of cardio (I still kept active with my day-to-day activities) until things got better.

Check out Dr. B G on the blog Animal Pharm, for her experience with AF and the leptin reset.Â  She disagrees with Dr. K on the order of things, but I guess it really matters if you have all the testing and expertise at your disposal at the outset.

Other experts, such as Schwarzbein, recommend no exercise and no ketogenic diet.

Your endo suggested cortisol, which I guess is a crutch, then again, crutches are handy when you break your leg.Â  When a doc recommends crutches, they don't intend for you do use them for life, do they?

### vkiernan — 2012-11-29T14:34:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-adrenal-fatigue-symptoms.5000/post-65851>

[http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/](http:///brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/)

Â 

I would say read the link if you haven't already and follow the protocol.Â  Do not exercise.Â  You already know it wipes you out.Â  I have had af for years but didn't really know it or understand it.Â  I cannot help you with the other stuff.Â 

I would think your last ASI would have told you the severity of it.Â  If you are tanked at all 4 times and your DHEA is tanked that is the last stage.Â  Jack says that it is not the adrenals unless Addison or Cushings(?) but the PVN according to theÂ blog linkÂ I posted above.Â  So if that's the case then you need really need to follow the light rules morning and evening.Â  You can look up af and find charts.Â  Some have 7 stages and some have 4.Â  My particular lab had 7 it showed my results and charted them so I had a clear visual.Â  I would say I still have it but I am slowly getting better.Â  Still really hard to stay awake in the evenings past 8 if I can even make it that long.Â Â 

How is your thyroid?Â  There is a protocol thatÂ calls for taking some form of t3 or t3/t4Â (NDT)Â except for slow release in the early hoursÂ when your body is making cortisol and this seems to help a lot of people right out of the gate.Â  The book is Recovering with T3 by Paul Robinson.Â  I can tell you it is helping me.Â  Slowly but surely. Â There is some info on the STTM website

I took bio-HC for a few months and at one point it did help but then that went away quickly.Â  I was taking between 27 and 32 a day.Â  I gained weight with it too.Â  Wasn't too happy about that.Â  I went off it to have some tests done and I was exhausted for weeks afterwards so I knew it was stopping my own adrenals from working.Â  I would think twice about starting it.Â 

I wish you the best in fixing yourself.Â  I understand where you are coming from and it is no picnic for sure.Â

### TheKid — 2012-11-29T15:36:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-adrenal-fatigue-symptoms.5000/post-65860>

Thanks for the replies.

I know I'm up the creek on this one. I've got Hashis and another autoimmune disease on top of all this as well. Thanks to modern medicine I am, however, not without a paddle. I am just trying to examine which paddle(s) to use and when. And what I am even more unsure of is how a possible mitochondrial problem would complicate all this.

With regard to the hydrocortisone, I would be interested in knowing if anyone has successfully weaned off it. If so, how did you wean and how long did it take?

### caroline — 2012-11-29T18:14:32+0200

### KiwiLauren — 2012-11-29T20:06:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-adrenal-fatigue-symptoms.5000/post-65895>

This is probably over simplistic, but isn't all disease the result of poor cellular signalling on some level? Â And all autoimmunity and other disease therefore pointing to impaired mitochondrial function? Â I think the d-Ribose (along with making sure you've got sufficient Mg, and maybe some l-carnitine and/or CoEnzyme Q10) can't be a bad idea... no matter how you slice it, supporting your mitochondria should support healing and health. ### Jude — 2012-11-30T06:45:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-adrenal-fatigue-symptoms.5000/post-65927>

**TheKid said
Â 

**But what worries me more than anything, is chest pain and shortness of breath. Two weeks ago, after doing some high intensity interval training, got really serious chest pain. It honestly felt like I had a rock in my chest instead of a heart. Of course I rushed to the cardiologist, and after a EKG and echo, I was once again told I was NOT having a heart attack.

That rock hard feeling is gone, but my days are up and down and literally all over the place. **Chest pain is frequent, as is mood. Sleep is erratic. And I have these terrible adrenaline rushes**. The rushes used to come once a night, but then increased to twice a night and even crept up during the day. I also am nearly always short of breath with any kind of physical activity, even walking. Since the cardio told me I surely don't have congestive heart failure, I am at a loss.**

Thanks.

Â 

The description of your symptoms that I've highlighted remind me somewhat of those I experienced over the last 5 yrs.

The following post of Dr K explaining the brain/heart/kidney relationship really explained the why/how they occur. Hope it's of some help-I found I had a great improvement with 2 weeks of a fish only diet.

Â 

For those who dont understand how brain function can cause heart rhythm issues here is the playbook again for you to review:**

The brain allows us to sense the epigenetics alterations in our environment well. I want you to now channel what I said about the heart and brain in Brain Gut 13 with respect to how the Na/K ATPase is affected in metabolic terms in all tissues. Then, I want you to think about the number one killer of all humans today: Heart disease. How does heart disease tie into this theory of whole body physiology?

If you begin to alter your blood glucose because of dietary or any other epigenetics signal (light or temp) and cause inflammation at a cellular level by eating badly or not being mindful of circadian cycles, you start secreting large amounts of insulin from beta cells to deal with it. This alters your Magnesium levels which activate the metabolically efficient Na/K ratio because of DHA as laid out in BG 13. When we have an altered Na/K (low) ratio, this in turn, simultaneously cause brain metabolism to ratched down because its function is directly coupled to the efficiency of the Na/K ATPase for our species. As the brainâ€™s metabolism falters, it begins to shunt energy to itself at the determent of other organs. It robs Peter to pay Paul. This physiologic bio energetic â€œstealingâ€￾ of energy alters functions everywhere in the body to satisfy the brains needs. As other organs fail slowly under this physiologic directive this further alters brain function from the excessive 60% energy boon created by the DHA/ Na/K ATPase, which results in your feeling less well with less energy and you have a chronic cognitive haze. Is this sounding familiar to anyone?
It is the modern human condition that most of us exist in. As this goes on chronically, brain function is eroded (the real source of adrenal fatigue in the PVN) because there is an excess of Na ions, which alter our homeostatic brain controls of the renin angiotensin system and this alters our Blood Pressure. The renin angiotensin system target organ is the kidneys. The kidneys control the electrolyte balance of the serum and ultimate of the CSF (ultrafiltrate of the serum) to further alter the function of our brain.

This slow erosion of our kidney function goes on undetected and slowly undermines our electrical systems balance. This implies that our declining kidney function, directly efffects our electrical system of our heart (conduction system AV node and sinus pacemaker) to become less optimal or â€œfoggyâ€￾ in its function too. The relays in the hearts electrical system is sticky and this cause all kinds of unusual heart rhythms. **This shows you why declining brain function always correlates with declining heart function. Modern medicine has never made this link but they know it exists. These organs are chemically and electrically tied together by the action of the Na/K ATPase, DHA and iodine at a foundational level physiologically in humans in our cell membranes.** This all results in the many types of cardiac dysrythmias we see clinically. This is an early sign of major bio-energenic shifts in cells all tied to oxidation.
When this goes on further brain function erodes and we can not sense it well. We, in essence, lose our primal sense of well being and eventually we develop an inability to sense vascular trouble before it happens clinically. This leads to a heart attack/palpatations/or heart failure.

Â
