I have heard that about margarine too.. Also diet drinks... Insects won't go anywhere near them.. I have done this experiment and I know it's true.

### diane — 2013-01-05T15:22:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-69462>

Yes, and it's amazing how all of the "healthy" people will eat margarine and drink diet drinks!

Sleep was not good last night. And I'm ticked!

Having the significant other home really does have some drawbacks - and we will have to work through. I was so tired and headachy I went to bed around 8, did some reading, then to sleep around 9. Woke up at around 2:30 and had to go to the bathroom. He was sitting in the living room with all the lights on - and it drove me so crazy I couldn't get back to sleep. Wow - it seems so crazy now that I've written it as to why I would spend time obsessing over this. But I did.

Got up at 9:30 or so in a bad mood. Walked the dogs and then played with them for a while after breakfast. Now in a good mood! See, spend some time and focus on someone else and the mood can change LOL. We are all really goofy together (I love my dogs) and it always makes me laugh.

Made up a bunch of burgers this morning for breakfasts. This grass fed ground beef I got is amazing. Nothing like what you get in the grocery store. I put some garlic powder, pepper and sea salt - delicious. Ate it with some bacon and Bubbies pickles.

Then, 45 minutes of CT in the tub. Way easier during the day! But i really don't have a choice during the week, so will do it in the day on the weekend when I can, and it will get easier at night as well. Today I submerged my hands and arms - I usually don't because I have my iPad while in the bath. But today I propped it on the toilet to watch the rest of my second viewing of the webinar (sorry Dr. K for propping you on the toilet!). Now they're so slow and feel like lead - even hard to type. I love this feeling! Wow! I'm not nearly as cold as I am at night. Wonder if I'll get the zoomies again! (With the energy I have - picture Sheldon on Big Bang Theory as The Flash).

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-05T20:14:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-69524>

Hey Diane ... my DH has been up late for the last week watching TV. He refuses to wear the blu blockers I bought for him because they "pinch." He's really been on a sugar binge and is eating wheat again. I. Give. Up. All I can do is lead by example. Nagging doesn't work. Lecturing doesn't work. AND he lied to me about a prescription that came in the mail the other day. he said it was for high blood pressure. I looked it up ... it's a statin.

What are ya gonna do? He's a grown man, 71, and I am not his mother (thank god). If he insists on doing stuff that will shorten what's left of his life I cannot make him change. All I can do is stand by his side, love him and let go of the rest.

I watch my iPad when I CT as well. It's a little easier for me as we have a \*cough cough\* "SUNKEN' tub. Which means the person who built this place cut a hole in the floor and dropped the tub in. It is the biggest PITA to clean you have ever encountered. I have to practically lie flat on the floor and then hold myself up with my left while I try and scrub with my right. You may now cover your ears for 10 seconds while I spew out a string of curse words worthy of a longshoreman.

ANYway, I bought a small stand for my iPad when I first got it ostensibly to use in the studio while working through tutorials at the torch. Well, that's my story and I'm stickin' to it! So I simply put the iPad on the stand, set it on the rug next to the tug and off I go. You might use a footstool to put yours on since you aren't below floor level in your tub. At least I hope you aren't! And I keep the door closed so the cats don't come in and knock it over. HTHs!

### endless — 2013-01-05T20:47:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-69528>

Hmm, I guess there are upsides to NOT having a DH

If I turn off a light, it STAYS turned off..lol

The ipad sounds much easier than my setup. I have to drag a kitchen chair into the bathroom to set my laptop on, plug in my speakers and balance them on the washing machine. I wouldn't trust myself holding the ipad over water though!

### diane — 2013-01-06T01:03:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-69551>

Darleen - you're absolutely right. They are grown men - I need to let go. It's my choice to be bothered or not, and being bothered just interrupts my sleep lol.

Yes, I'm not sunken, I'm even with the ground. But the toilet seat does work great because it's right beside the tub. I'm in a pretty small condo. And it's not the cats - but one of my dogs always does come in and check on me when I'm in the tub LOL. Nosy bugger.

### diane — 2013-01-06T18:55:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-69656>

We went out for a friend's birthday last night, so got home kinda late. Ate a bit late too. But hey, we don't get invited out very much, so I was happy to go!

Got up super early (ok it was 6:45 on a Sunday lol) to take the dogs on a hike. We were a bit north of the city, so there was more snow, which they loved. Plus, walking through the snow felt good on the legs! Especially the hills.

Because I was up so early, I didn't leave myself enough time for BAB, so made my bulletproof green tea and was off. I'm feeling that now though as I'm hungry and almost craving. Do I hear fat!! LOL. Had a nap when I got home. I have no guilt whatsoever napping after a hike. The 3 of us (me and the dogs) must have been snoring up a storm. My significant other didn't get up to go with us - and he was still in bed when we got home. I guess that's my Type A personality - I have to get up and doing things most days. I have some days where I do very little - but those are pretty few.

I"ve really enjoyed the forum lately - so many new people contributing and I love their perspective. I always look forward to their being new posts! Such a geek, I know. Plus, on top of that I've been going back and re-reading many of the blog posts, starting with the ones recommended in the webinar. It's great - and yes, I am getting a better understanding now. Still can't explain things though. That's my goal!

No tub today - but will spot CT later with my ice packs. I don't know if it's because of the CT or because it wasn't all that cold today (+2 degrees C) but I was sweating on our hike. When we got back to the Jeep, my hair was soaking wet! I want the furnace to turn on like Darleen has!!

### diane — 2013-01-07T13:50:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-69753>

I'm finally getting my latest labs today! After my consult, it took 2 months to get in to see the anti-aging doc, another month + to see my doctor to order the labs, and another 2-3 weeks to wait for the right time in my cycle. Another week or two for results, Christmas holidays (where no one is open). And hopefully they're faxing today! I'm very excited.

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T20:03:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-69795>

Fingers crossed for your results!

### diane — 2013-01-08T00:38:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-69816>

Well, I got them back. Not sure what to make of them yet. Some things up, some things down. I don't have the Vitamin D results - and I paid for those! So will have to call tomorrow as it may just not have come through. Big D'OH - I didn't get Estradiol or Progesterone. What?? Somehow I thought those were on the list, so may have to go back. My hs CRP is 1.2 - so not great. IGF-1 is down from a year and a half ago. Oops. B12 is off the chart - zinc is low. DHEA-S is up slightly. No clue what all this means, but will have to find out soon!

### endless — 2013-01-08T13:02:33+0200

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-08T15:57:26+0200

First on my high estrogen day and then 10 days later on the high progesterone day. Shrug. Didn't ask why (most docs don't like to be bothered with explaining the little things to patients) but I assumed there was a need to read both levels that way.

### diane — 2013-01-08T19:30:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-69959>

The latest were all blood tests. I did a salivary panel last March with melatonin, cortisol, DHEA - so should probably do that one again as well.

Didn't have time to call about my Vitamin D today - will have to call tomorrow.

My sleep has been off the last 3 days - mainly because I've been out at night, and haven't been following the light. Wow, do I feel the difference. And it ain't pretty people. Just shows me how important it is!

### diane — 2013-01-11T00:28:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70263>

It's been a couple of days since I posted. Last night the internet went down. The internet! Kinda crazy how odd it felt to be disconnected....yet freeing at the same time.

Hmmmm....the more I learn the more I get confused about what I should be doing. Other than epi-paleo and CT - not sure of next steps. I need to figure out how to move forward. While I've made some great strides forward, I want to make more. The anti-aging doctor that I'm seeing didn't see it as a big deal that I didn't get my estradiol or progesterone tested. To me that's a red flag! Those were important to me.....I still can't believe we missed that LOL. Soon enough. I have an appointment with her on Monday, but now I'm interested as to what she's really going to tell me.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T14:47:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70315>

I know how difficult it is to get your doc to listen. I have been very lucky in that I have encountered a few who actually treated me as though I have a brain in my head. But they always disappear on me! Which is why I"m dragging my feet on finding a new one. And I need to see someone because I think my estrogen needs a bump. Sore nipples again. I've changed nothing in my protocol so am baffled as to why this should be. The OD I saw originally told me when this happened 3 months into the program that I needed more estrogen, which really surprised me, but I did what he said and the problem resolved. But it does mean I need an adjustment to my scrip.

sigh. So not looking forward to this. Finding a new doc that is.

### vkiernan — 2013-01-11T22:45:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70367>

> **DarleenMB said**
>
> I know how difficult it is to get your doc to listen. I have been very lucky in that I have encountered a few who actually treated me as though I have a brain in my head. But they always disappear on me! Which is why I"m dragging my feet on finding a new one. And I need to see someone because I think my estrogen needs a bump. Sore nipples again. I've changed nothing in my protocol so am baffled as to why this should be. The OD I saw originally told me when this happened 3 months into the program that I needed more estrogen, which really surprised me, but I did what he said and the problem resolved. But it does mean I need an adjustment to my scrip.
> sigh. So not looking forward to this. Finding a new doc that is.

Darleen, how about less progesterone? That is if you are taking it. I have the high estrogen thing going on and sometimes I take too much P and that seems to be what happens and then I back off the P and all is good. CT'ing will reduce your estrogen so less P should be needed.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T23:34:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70372>

Vickie that is a great explanation, thanks. I did NOT know that about CT or if I did it went in one ear and out the other. My dosing is carefully laid out, btw. I'm winding down this cycle on the progesterone but next cycle I'll drop back by one line a day and see if that helps.

### diane — 2013-01-12T12:34:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70398>

Yes and you're the queen of CT Darleen!

Man, it's been harder than I thought having someone else around. My whole pattern and groove have been thrown off lol. Although I can say that I have seen him turn out the lights and he's making an effort. So I have to make a decision as to what kind of lifestyle I want and what I'm willing to give up.

Like last night, I came home, we couldn't figure out what we wanted for dinner, he wanted pizza, so we ended up ordering it. Ayeyiyi (not sure if that came out the way I intended it lol). This morning, instant pain in my back, legs and my hands are swollen. But it was MY decision last night - I could have made a different one. The hard part is the next day, I continue to crave carbs and sugar, and almost gave in for breakfast. But instead, I reheated some of my hamburger, roast beef and steak (had bits of leftovers in the fridge) with some bacon grease, added lime juice and sprinkled cilantro on it. Also made bulletproof coffee instead of my green tea (although know when I make coffee I realize I love the green tea more - which is really huge for me!). And I feel better already. I really believe that breakfast is key - so to get back on track with breakfast definitely helps me during the day!

I've also been trying to track my blood sugar levels, but I am somehow an idiot at measuring it LOL. I must not prick my finger properly because I can never get the right amount of blood on the stick. LOL. Guess I need more practice!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-12T14:05:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70412>

Well Diane it is great that you went with the BAB this morning because there were times, at least for me, that I would have used last night's pizza as an excuse to binge. So good for you!!! If we get pizza, which is rare now, I order it with lots of bacon and beef (my favorites) and just eat the top, leaving the crust. Not optimal but better than eating wheat! I think I solidified my determination not to eat wheat after having 3 mini crackers at a party prior to the holidays. I was so bloated for days afterwards that I thought "never again"!
Geez, I'm long-winded today!

I also looked into those Lands End pants that Darleen mentioned - I think I'm going to try them!

From my 1/4 GF beef, we got 2 huge bones. These commercials that say that 80% of dogs over 3 have dental issues are driving me crazy - obviously they're not eating what they're designed to eat! And they're trying to sell dentabones to fix it. Yikes. Here's my dog's toothbrush lol.

### diane — 2013-01-13T23:49:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70620>

Ah Sunday. Great day today. I finished watching the webinar. It's a great one! If not a bit over my head, although I did get a lot out of it. But this gives me something to aspire to - to work through a lot of the biochemistry to understand it better.

I spent the afternoon working with dogs, which is my favourite thing to do. I think that's where I feel my best - I always have energy, I have fun and it's so mentally stimulating for me.

Watching the Golden Globes. Robert Downey Jr. is just hot. That's all.

### diane — 2013-01-14T13:36:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70682>

Woke up with a headache today and realized it's been a LONG time since that has happened. Another success story. It's a headache coupled with a really draggy feeling.

So I always go back to see what's changed and what could be causing it, and I'm pretty sure that it has do to with the light, and the fact that I've been eating a bit later again and my significant other always wants one light on. I think it's been destroying my sleep again, so time to tighten up. The light makes such a difference - but I really think that I notice it more once I've stopped doing it, as I didn't realize just how much better I was feeling. Maybe it's a combination of eating and the light, but honestly, the eating hasn't been that far off (a few things, but not consistently) while the light for me has been the biggest change.

Am also going to up the iodine - having bone broth with seaweed for lunch. See if that can help too.

I have my appointment with my anti-aging doctor this afternoon. She's apparently putting together a whole plan for me. Hmmm....wonder how much I'm going to have to argue against LOL. I'm assuming there will be lots of exercise in there. I know that she doesn't have my estrogen/progesterone results because I was a bonehead and forgot lol, so I know no BHRT (which was the reason I called in the first place), but possibly some thyroid support. I have to book my consult with Dr. K - but want to have my Vitamin D results before I do, and they seem to have gone missing, even though I paid out of pocket for those. I could get my P and E tested and pay out of pocket - but that seems kind of silly because it would be covered. But now have to wait until I see my dr in Feb, then wait for day 21 of my cycle - that will make it almost 7 months to get this tested. Jeez! Gotta make better notes to myself to make phone calls lol.

### caroline — 2013-01-14T17:45:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70729>

Good luck with your anti aging Doc Diane - I hope it goes smoothly

### diane — 2013-01-14T19:14:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70750>

Thanks Caroline! It was fine. I think I just need to adjust my expectations about what I expect from them lol.

She gave me pages of recommendations. Most of which I just smiled and said ok and inside I said "toss". Especially the nutrition ones. The advice is to add 1-2 servings of soy PER DAY. Soy! I haven't heard that one in a while. Reduce red meat to 3 times a week. Fish 3 times a week. Toss.

Exercise - I walk the dogs at least an hour a day, and we walk fast. Apparently I need to be walking fast enough so I can't get a sentence out. Huh? And add resistance training.

Supplements - multivitamin (toss). Vitamin E, D, C, calcium/ mag citrate (toss). Fish oil (toss). Chastetree for my periods - and if that doesn't work, progesterone cream (hmmm, would have preferred to start with that!).

She is putting me on thyroid supp (something that starts with P, has both T3 and T4). Low doses, test in 4 weeks and adjust. So that part is good. Prescription for DHEA which is great - although only 10 mg/day. Wasn't concerned that I didn't have the estradiol and progesterone as she goes by how I feel. That worries a bit.

But I think kind of typical as to what's going on out there - she's actually probably more progressive than most. Guess I'm not typical!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-14T20:14:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70761>

She sounds world's ahead of my doc but soy!!! Really!!! I didn't think Anyone was still recommending the stuff! Great news about the thyroid meds.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-14T20:42:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70764>

Maybe you need to find that article out on the internets that explains why soy is so bad and just give her a copy to read. Ya never know.

### diane — 2013-01-14T20:51:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70768>

I think she saw the look on my face, and told me that she's searched pub med and can't find where it says soy is bad, as long as it's organic non GMO soy. I'm still not buying it, though. She told me it helps build breast tissue and manage estrogen. ??

Especially 2 servings a day. So what she's recommending is soy and vegetables, supplemented once a day with either fish or meat. Yeesh.

I see this as an opportunity for me to really kill this thing - make the changes here and go back and surprise her with what's going on! Wouldn't that be awesome!!!

### caroline — 2013-01-14T20:55:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70772>

I think - as others have said - you have to pretend to play along and nudge her in the direction you need to go. Honestly - compounding pharmacies are a treasure trove of information. When I picked up my progesterone the other evening - I saw a pharmacist I had never seen before. She was asking me a lot of questions and I was kinda looking at her. She wanted to know about the Doc that had written the script etc. etc. etc. We finally wound up talking about Jack kruse - she was very interested. It turns out the reason she was asking was - she has a new customer moving here from Sydney and she was asking about Docs here and BHRT and she wondered how I had happened onto all this info.

### endless — 2013-01-14T23:06:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70794>

I agree Caroline...I ended up talking to the compounding pharmacist about Dr.K as well.

So many people are still buying into that soy thing! Makes me crazy. In my nutrition classes one of the things that was stressed is that soy has been used for thousands of years in Asia...yes, but not as a food, as a soil amendment! It was a green crop planted to enrich the soil. When it was eaten it was fermented, in small quantities, and always with fish (!)

Love the bone...my dog eats bones all the time but her favorite are the small rounds of marrow bone so she can clean out the middle easily.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-15T00:01:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70806>

Yup, what Krista just said. That was the information I had read somewhere out on the nets. I was stunned. It is just not healthy for people IMNSHO. But like wheat it is in practically EVERYthing. I've pretty much moved away from anything with an ingredient list on the label.

### kzimm — 2013-01-15T16:42:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70906>

Hi Diane,

Saw you asked this in another thread..."I find that I also have to pee a lot during CT, and then even for a couple of hours after. I wonder if it's related to that??"

This info was in the CT 6 blog comments:

Question: Dr. Kruse. I feel like a million bucks! So many good changes, and it can only get better. Just out of curiosity, can you tell me why I pee like a racehorse after getting out of the cold tub or after using an ice pack on my menopot. Thank you ever so much.
Answer: its the efect on ADH in hypothalamus as it rewires.....it will pass too......mammals do not pee when they hibernate. Neither will you eventually

### diane — 2013-01-15T16:54:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70911>

Why thank you so much! I had missed that!

I do notice that I don't have to get up in the night anymore - so that's a great sign as well. Plus good for the sleep. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### diane — 2013-01-15T20:57:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70956>

Definitely felt better today- the headache was gone. I doubled up on what I usually do in the morning - ie. twice the CoQ10 (although I was only taking 200 mg), double the L-carnatine, double the D-rhibose. Am testing when I should take it during the day to increase. It's a bit hard to take stuff to work, so I'm testing whether it will work at night, or keep me up too late. I definitely notice the boost in energy!

Plus, last night I made sure the lights were all out, bed at about 10:20 - and I felt so much better. So yes, for me, light is key.

One of my co-workers came by my desk today and commented on how "bright" I looked - and she wasn't talking about my clothes! She was talking about the glow I think. So I talked a bit to her about what I'm doing. And she has many of the same things - crazy cravings, late night eating. I sent her a link to the Leptin prescription. And she has a daughter with Aspergers - so I'm sure much of the information here will help.

Spurred on by everyone else saying GITDT (get in the damn tub), I just ran the water. CT bonus - doesn't matter if you flush the toilet while the water is running! I actually measured the temp and it's 46 degrees right out of the tap. I knew it was cold! Damn! Good thing is wasn't just my imagination, or me being a big wuss LOL.

Will have to read the new blog. Now I have earthing and grounding to learn about on top of mitochondria. Holy crap - I'm going to be busy. Am very interested as to how I can learn about earthing as it relates to animals, after seeing Shijin's post in the Ask Jack. Hmmmm....am going to have to start testing my theories. May be why the dogs that do lots of hikes in the forest are much more grounded - plus the mental stimulation and socialization. And my dogs as soon as I open the door, they will go outside and lie on the concrete. I wonder how much I'm affecting them by living downtown in the concrete jungle from an earthing perspective. I do lots with them, we walk an hour minimum every day - but now I'm thinking. Hmmmm....

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-15T23:52:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-70987>

youre affecting them a lot. And the next blog post may really open your eyes and tell you where you live really does matter for reasons you might not have a clue exist.

### caroline — 2013-01-16T03:32:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71000>

good on you for just getting in the damn tub Diane!

### diane — 2013-01-16T15:27:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71070>

I'm finding that if I just start filling the tub and tell myself 10 minutes, I'm more likely to get in. Of course I do longer than 10 minutes (last night was 30) - the 10 minutes just gets me in. Once I'm in, in 10 minutes I'm getting "comfortable" - so might as well stay!

Wasn't too cold getting out - but still not burning like some people are. I'm sure it's not too far away! I've also been having the windows down driving to work - that's a rush!

So, adding the CoQ10, d-rhibose and L-carnatine at dinner time last night did not impact me getting to sleep. Of course, I did CT as well, which usually makes me sleepy. I also doubled what I was doing again this morning, and felt more alert. My nose is running, my eyes are running and it feels like I'm coughing up junk. So it looks like some stuff is clearing out, which would be amazing!

More and more I realize that I need to move from where I am. Not totally out of the city, but definitely out of downtown. Probably north of the city. I've been feeling this way for a few months especially but started thinking a couple of years ago. But more and more I think it's the right thing to do. I just have to prep myself for a lifestyle change, but I'm up for it.

### caroline — 2013-01-17T05:48:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71178>

It sounds like you and the dogs would really thrive in the country ....I hope you are able to do it - will probably add years to all of your lives

Now, Diane, I have a confession to make ---- after all that worry about the correct time for the Q&A ....... I didn't check the date close enough! I thought it was tomorrow!!! I missed the first 40 minutes - I actually would have missed all of it - except I went on line and thought I should register....
stupid, stupid, stupid!!!

### diane — 2013-01-19T20:57:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71710>

Been a couple of days since I updated!

Crazy day on Thursday. Crazy because I had crazy carb cravings which I haven't had for a while. I had bread and ice cream. Yes, that I did. Really impacted my sleep too. I want to get to the bottom of this because it's driving me crazy. It usually happens towards the end of the week. Maybe there's a correlation between number of days in the office and cravings. It is always late in the day - never morning or lunch time. I think I've been eating enough for breakfast, but maybe I should actually start calculating protein grams. I also realized just how much I eat when I have the cravings, and how much less I eat when I don't. Yes, I have the BAB, but lunch and dinner are fairly small. And normally I don't eat any snacks, unless the cravings strike. They have been fewer and further between though, which is good news. But I would love to say bye bye.

Took a drive today to a place that has amazing raw food for dogs. All whole raw - they had everything from beef, lamb, pork, chicken, duck, turkey, elk, bison. They had bones, meat, ground, offal, tripe. Amazing! I tried to get some lamb hearts for myself, but they were out. I also use the bones for my bone broth lol. I have enough beef in the freezer, so it was just turkey, lamb, tripe and offal that I got. And an elk, bison mix as well. This should last for a while!

I took a detour on the way home, looking at places we may want to move. Not sure exactly where, but I do think it will be sooner rather than later!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-19T21:16:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71720>

I'm not expert on curing the cravings but I used to binge and my cravings would always be late in the day, always. If I ever slipped on the BAB I could guarntee I'd have cravings. At one point I needed 65 grams of protein with loads of fat for BAB and needed this for quite sometime. This was the only thing that stopped the cravings for me. The. Only. Thing. If I get a craving now I find a source of fat as fast as I can, usually a spoonful or two of coconut butter does the trick. But, again, the BAB with lots of protein was key for me.
That raw food place sounds great. My dog would love to visit you!

### endless — 2013-01-19T21:49:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71724>

I have carb cravings on days I'm not busy enough....like my bored brain is looking for a 'party in my mouth' to liven things up a bit

I keep pork rinds, roasted peanuts (yes I know but it works) or organic unsweetened 'Jello' to snack on and it helps a lot.

I wish my dog food place had such a good selection! I buy a pre made mixed raw food that strains my budget but is all good high quality human grade ingredients. I don't think I'd eat it myself though

### diane — 2013-01-19T21:59:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71730>

Hmmmm....good points ladies. Paula - maybe I do need to increase protein even more on the BAB. The other thing is that I get less diligent as the week goes on, so I may skimp a bit more on the BAB. Will plan better this week and see how it goes. Also need to get some coconut butter.

Krista - I do all my own raw. I can't afford pre-mixed raw for my two 85 lbs dogs, so I just get raw meat, portion it out and away we go. I don't give them vegetables, but in the summer they eat grass and dirt. LOL.

Paula - yes your dog would love visiting me LOL! When I have clients' dogs over, they usually get a taste of the raw. They get excited when they come!

### caroline — 2013-01-20T01:56:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71760>

I used to need upwards of 80gr or even more to kill the cravings - if that helps anyone...

### diane — 2013-01-20T11:41:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71826>

Thanks Caroline. Although like Darleen, I struggle some days to get in the 50g!

I do know that I really have to be diligent about when I eat dinner the night before, because if it's too late, I not only feel it in my sleep, but I don't even feel like eating breakfast. Which then causes a huge cascade down the line.

This morning I had 2 x 4oz grass fed beef burgers with a fried egg on top and a Bubbies pickle. My bulletproof green tea (which is vanilla green tea from Tealish - all natural, green tea leaves, yum!- plus butter, coconut oil, some sea salt and 2 drops of stevia mixed in my Vitamix for 7 seconds). And drank a little bit of the Bubbies juice for the probiotics. Will see how this goes. I made a batch of burgers while I was at it, so will have some for this week. I will get bacon as well as a couple of slices will help get up the protein count.

I know I can add fat - but somehow I'm on an avocado strike - maybe too much lately? I find I go in waves - can eat a lot of something, then can't eat it for a while.

Hmmm...had an epiphany about feeding my dogs lol. I ususally give them a small amount in the morning and more at night. Probably should be reversing that - a lot in the morning with a bit at night! Also bought some salmon bellies yesterday, and the little prince and princess won't eat it unless it's cooked. I have found out before that they would rather starve than eat raw fish. I felt silly "cooking" up their breakfast - but a little heat on each side and down it went!

### diane — 2013-01-20T11:50:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71828>

Oh - and I"m getting dangerously close to trying the MHS. LOL. I actually bought mackeral at the pet place yesterday - but they are small, so will use the whole fish. How does this count towards protein, or do you just add this as a "freebie" on top?

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2013-01-20T12:38:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71838>

Don't think too much!! Go ahead and try it!!

I hope you have a good blender, because when I tried it, there was chunks of fish in the not-so-smooth smoothie, which made it a lot enjoyable. I guess it counts for protein if you put the whole thing in it.

When I don't eat enough in the morning I get cravings also at the end of the day. I used to have up to 85g of protein to kill them!! And add some fat!! I like to make myself a tea with my BAB, and I blend it ith CO. I used different kind of tea or even herbal tea from David's Tea...Yummy!!

### endless — 2013-01-20T13:04:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71845>

The big sardines I buy have a nutrient panel on the bag...each fish is listed as 20g of protein. I use 2 big ones or 3 smaller ones for each smoothie and count that as my BAB. You won't be hungry for anything else right then, I don't think. I add lemon juice, salt and parsley, and enough water to make it work. Blend it for longer than you think you need to....until you stop hearing little sharp noises coming from the Vitamix. (Bones, maybe?) I use a straw to drink it....tastes fishy but not terrible.

### diane — 2013-01-20T14:25:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71858>

I have a Vitamix so blending shouldn't be a problem. I love that thing! Found it on kijiji last year, barely used, but a lot cheaper than new!

Ok - tomorrow am. But I hate parsley, so will go with lemon juice and cilantro!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-20T14:29:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71859>

Yup ... up that protein ladies. That is the mistake I have been making the last several weeks and why, I now realize, my sleep has been off and my estrogen wasn't getting where it's supposed to go. Damn, I can sure be stupid sometimes.

Still not ready for a fish smoothy. Of course, getting real fresh whole fish here is a challenge so that's my excuse for today.

### caroline — 2013-01-20T19:28:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-71917>

Yep - lots of protein - but I really think you can make up the difference with fat - much easier to get down. I posted this once quite awhile ago now and Dr. K. commented that that is what he does and it is much easier.

### diane — 2013-01-21T09:11:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72002>

Well, I did it. Yes, today marks the first MHS (mackerel head smoothie). Not so much the heads - but 3 small full mackerels. At first, I was a big chicken. I put off getting out of bed, once I made it I let it sit for a while. It took me some time to work up to it. But it wasn't nearly as bad as I thought it would be. Just fishy. Need to add a bit more lemon and cilantro (I used a half lemon but could have used more). And it made A LOT! Holy cow! About a full large glass and a half. Maybe I should do 2 tomorrow and work back up to 3. I'm very proud of myself!

But just to show how crazy I am, I put a little bit in each of the dog's dishes - and they wouldn't touch it! Hmmmm...what does that say if the dogs wouldn't even eat it! LOL!

Now to figure out lunch lol. Was going to do salmon - but maybe I'm fished out for the day! { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72009>

LOL Diane, good for you. To me it says that even your dogs are pretty conditioned.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-21T13:04:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72053>

Diane are these fresh or were they frozen? and might I just add ... gack.

### diane — 2013-01-21T13:11:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72055>

They were frozen. I ran them under water to thaw a little bit - then blended them. Didn't help that my significant other was up and gagging - which made me put it off LOL.

Jack I can't wait to read EMF-2. I'm sure that I've screwed them up - even though I think I've been doing so much to make their lives better. But now I'll know better.

Funny thing is, I would be more likely to change my lifestyle if I knew what I was doing to them vs. to me. Kinda crazy eh! Although I have made some radical changes to myself - ala MHS breakfasts.

### endless — 2013-01-21T13:24:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72059>

> It means the dogs have been radically changed by their keeperâ€¦â€¦.Reality bomb.

Maybe....more likely is that they don't like lemon. Mine doesn't, either.

Good job! I don't do them more that about once a week but I always feel good when I do!

### diane — 2013-01-21T14:39:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72080>

I do feel really "clear" today.

Then I had a delicious lunch - raw salmon with lime juice, olive oil, grated ginger, little bit of balsamic vinegar, onions and sea salt. Wow - delicious!

My guys don't like raw fish at all - I have to cook it. So maybe I have screwed them LOL.

### diane — 2013-01-21T19:26:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72150>

Hmmm....I need to put this here because I think at some point I will want to reflect back.

Today I feel different and I'm trying to articulate what it is.

Maybe it's the MHS I had for breakfast. No, it's not the fact of what was in it, but that to me it actually felt like time for me to try it. When I first read people doing tho, I thought they were crazy and didn't think I would ever try it. I didn't feel stress or less than anyone - just wasn't something I was ready to try. Nor was eating raw meat. But I have been on a journey for over 2 years, and somehow at this point I knew that this was the next step for me.

Maybe it's because by doing this, then my amazing salmon lunch, I have had ZERO cravings today. Zero. Cravings have been driving me crazy. Now that's what's been causing me stress! Back to university, eating 5-6 slices of bread after dinner. Then on Weight Watchers where I would eat as much of a zero or low point snack after dinner. Whatever it was, I have had very few periods where I haven't been interested in eating, and mostly I willed my way through it. Today I feel calm for some reason.

Maybe it's reading EMF-2. Something felt out of place, yet right. LIke I know I'm in for changes, but somehow this validated it. It's funny, a few months back someone asked where I was happiest. I left and couldn't really give an answer. Then a few days later, it struck me. I'm happiest when I'm outside. That's it. Very simple. It can be the ocean, the mountain, the forest or the pavement. I feel happier outside. That was a huge revelation to me. And now I know why. I've been looking on and off at moving for a couple of years now. More so in the last 3 or 4 months. Now I know why. I've been wondering why I start a job and gain 20 lbs in less than 3 months - what did I do wrong, why couldn't I control myself. Now I know why.

Maybe it's a meeting I had this afternoon where a mentor of mine outlined a plan that really falls in line with a lot of the stuff I've been thinking about lately. The timing was uncanny. He does a lot of this stuff out of instinct, and I told him a bit of what I've been reading about and thinking, and I believe that he got it. Now I may have opportunities that I wouldn't have considered before.

Maybe it's because I have new eyes reading some of the things people write. I'm not as open as some, or as ethereal as others, but I appreciate it all. I believe I'm smart, but not intellectual as some people are. I used to feel bad about myself in all of this, but not anymore. Sometimes I feel bad for the others because I see that they can't see. Maybe I can have a small part in helping them, maybe they don't want help. But I'm not the type that gives up easily.

Maybe because it's because I'm enjoying the journey I'm on. I like being the outlier. I like being the crazy one jumping in cold baths, because I know what it does for me while other people just think it's weird or uncomfortable. But then, they get a spark and they think, maybe it's not so crazy after all. That's when I smile.

Anyways, it's been an interesting day for sure. I love the people here and what I get from them. I find this to be one of the most interesting communities I've come across - not just in what's discussed, but how it's discussed. People having real discussion without bashing or getting angry. Must be all the seafood.

Man - there's some people I would love to serve a boatload of seafood to! LOL.

Today I thank everyone here for what they've contributed to my life. But especially to Jack for what he's shared with us and what he's created here. Yes, what I didn't know DID hurt me. When I say "I'm learning" - I try to learn something from everyone and that's what I love about today.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-21T19:41:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72154>

Good for you!!! Good for MHS and articulating what is making a difference for you. I'm not even going to pretend I'm ready for the MHS because I, like your boyfriend, gagged at the thought. But all you MHS drinkers are inspiring!

### diane — 2013-01-21T20:15:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72168>

Oh, I won't lie. I actually woke up in the night thinking about it! But I had committed in my mind LOL.

Actually just did some grounding while I was walking the dogs. It's not so easy to find a spot where I live, and can't do it on my patio because we're above a parking garage. So just went into a park on the way home and lied down on the ground. There's no snow, but it's -8 C out, so the ground was cold enough, and I only wear a fairly light coat and pants. I've yet to pull out my winter coat this year! I had the dogs with me, and what they did was really cool. One sat facing the same way I was, the other stood behind facing the other way. They were watching my back, and each watching a different way. It's so cool to watch what they do. Then they played for a little bit off leash. My guys are great - I can walk them anywhere offleash - even downtown!

This.........is why I do what I do. Making change becomes much easier when you know the why.......the how gets easier. Magically all the barriers you put up.....fall. Look at Paula response above.......when she reads EMF 2 and see what she wrote with her old understanding........do you think she is going to leave any electrons on the table? When you think about peoples struggles..........and you know why......and they dont and wont see it? Then you see a way to make them see it.......and they discover it for themselves........and their old eyes......become new eyes.......suddenly what you wrote yesterday seem foreign to you in 3 hours........That is beautiful............some may not understand it Diane.......but I do and I am very glad you wrote it. It is the fuel that will keep me tinkering. There can be no try in doing.......their must be doing in doing and learning from our failures and not being blocked by our fears created in our head for any reason at all. You got that message today. I like that.

I love the stuff you write - you are a very compassionate and caring woman and you are one smart cookie as well!

### ashryn — 2013-01-22T06:22:41+0200

### diane — 2013-01-22T08:32:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72270>

Thanks all. Ashryn - you're plenty growed up! And you're smart to avoid many of the dumb mistakes that I've made to get me in this place. Of course, if you don't know, they're not dumb. Someone very wise told me rather than to beat myself up about what I've done or not done realize that I did the best I could with what I knew then. And that's what I strive to do every day - the best I can with what I know now. Now I just know a bit more than I did then! LOL.

This morning was one of the first that I didn't have to drag myself out of bed. Yes, I have noticed an overall difference since yesterday. Was it the MHS or lying on the ground? Don't know, but whatever it is, it's good. I hate the feeling of not being able to get up in the morning.

No MHS today. Made a pork shoulder in the slow cooker yesterday, so had that with avocado for breakfast today. Caroline - I think you're right, it was way too big! If it had been a glass, I would have been better, but holy that was a lot to get down!

### diane — 2013-01-23T21:57:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72597>

I just may have a little crazy in me.....

This morning on the way to work, it was -10 degrees C, and I put down the driver and passenger windows halfway while driving down the highway - I could see my breath (heater off of course). Then kept yelling "woohoo!" cause it felt so darn exhilarating. Actually I didn't feel crazy, but man it felt great! I find that driving to work that way starts me out in a great mood for the day!

Haven't had cravings the last few days. Oh what a relief. Have been eating my salmon ceviche for lunch - and today I wasn't that hungry for lunch. But I did have some hunger for breakfast which I haven't had for a while.

Have slept a bit better the last couple of nights too. And having dreams which I can't quite remember, but remember that I was dreaming. So I take that as a good sign as well.
.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-23T23:27:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72621>

"I put down the driver and passenger windows halfway while driving down the highway â€“ I could see my breath (heater off of course). Then kept yelling "woohoo!" cause it felt so darn exhilarating. "

That's great. I wonder if I can do that in our winter - well not the -10, we are lucky to get -1 here. But I do find myself thinking, "woohoo" when I can cope with 40C (105F) without totally wilting. How can getting cold make the heat more tolerable?

### diane — 2013-01-24T08:52:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72637>

I don't know Aussie nana - but you're right. Not only do I not feel the cold as much, I don't feel the heat either! It's amazing isn't it! Probably why I'm still wearing my fall coat in -14 degree weather and everyone else has their Canada Goose jackets on as soon as it's under 10 degrees celsius. LOL.

### diane — 2013-01-26T01:45:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72686>

Ah- I see the forum has switched! Darn - lost my comment about everyone needing to do a Woohoo!  Believe it or not, I do that sometimes while out walking the dogs. I may look crazy, but I'm havin' fun people.

Started my thyroid medication today (it's bio-identical - but the bottle just says "thyroid" so I'm really not sure what it is. How weird is that?). I could call the compounding pharmacy to find out. Even the info and directions that they sent don't have the name on it. I thought the doctor said it started with a p and it's both T3 and T4. I have a month's worth, then I'm testing to see where I'm at. She also wrote me a prescription for DHEA, although I'm only taking 10 mg. But will see how it goes.

Not sure I feel anything from it today. Not sure today will be a good gauge, given the bit of partying last night and overindulging in red wine.  I actually feel ok today - and it was fun!

### diane — 2013-01-27T16:56:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-72867>

Day 3 of thyroid meds - have not noticed any difference. Other than by having to wait 30 minutes to eat after taking it, my breakfast has now been pushed to almost an hour. Not happy about that. Does anyone know if the 30 minutes is actually necessary? May have to put that in the T3 thread.

Since I have very few grounding opportunities, being it's middle of winter in downtown Toronto, last night while walking the dogs, I pulled over into a small park, let them off leash and laid down on the ground. The youngest dog would run up to me and stop just as he got to me, spraying snow all over (think grade school when the boys would spray the snow all over the girls when stopping on hockey skates. I then learned how to do that too by the way). Had a laugh the first couple of times, then got them to sit by me. I hear a man yell out "Are you ok?" then realized how crazy I must have looked (not that I care). Realized that it's much better to have THAT reaction than have something wrong and no one say anything. I laughed all the way home.

I like the discussions that are happening over the EMF-2 blog here, as it's making me think. Not just about the information that was presented, but it's interesting how different people assess and assimilate information. It's made me think a lot about my own style, which is more to use my gut. If something is plausible to me, even if I don't understand it completely, I can buy into it as I work through and test to see if I believe it works. Some people question and poke holes in it. I appreciate those people because it helps me understand if I get or not, or what it proves. Like Kathylu said in one of threads - if I think it could work and I can test it easily, I will, and see how it works for me. That's how I've gotten along here so far, and why I keep staying (because it's been working for me). I think it's a much bigger nut to chew because it is paradigm shifting. Somehow I feel that we are going along the right path and onto something big. But that's my gut talking now, I could never convince anyone scientifically.

That's not my deal - I'm a "show it" type of person. I hate when someone selling me something (which can be an idea) relies too much on science, because I also believe that there's so much that science doesn't know. I hear this all the time in my "second" job - and my methods are very different from most. Because I see them work and I have experience with many others who see the same. Don't get me wrong - it's not that I don't believe in many things with science - I just believe that the right question isn't always asked.

I like what you said Diane, it kinda sums up how I feel too! One additional thought came to mind when I read the end of your post...although I believe in science too, science is ofter "persuaded" by those paying for the science!

Sometimes I disregard it...like in the case of my EMF protector pendant that is not based on conventional wisdom science. I am going with my gut on that one.

I'm still working on grounding ideas for outside....it's just too cold for standing in the snow for more than 5 minutes! Maybe some wool socks....

### caroline — 2013-01-27T23:57:05+0200

### diane — 2013-01-29T00:16:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-73071>

I posted this in the Optimal reset thread, but want to put in my journal as well.

Going back to why I started on this journey, much of it (besides weight which is the visual representation I suppose) was runny eyes and constant congestion. Well, let me back up. It started with low energy, foggy thinking, general feeling of horribleness and bitchiness. Sound appealing?  Somethings got better, some didn't. And then I noticed my eyes were running so badly that I had rashes on my face and my sinuses were always congested. And I have little to no sense of smell (sometimes a blessing).

I have noticed that over the past couple of weeks, my eyes have barely run (one day when I had carbs, although it was gluten free) and I don't feel the pressure in my sinuses. This is pretty crazy. It had been getting a bit better (I'm sure the sleep has helped) but it seems to have gotten even better when I increased the CoQ10, D-rhibose and L-carnatine. I did it all at one time, so not sure exactly which one may have helped, or possibly all. Also increased mag, although still just using mag malate as I haven't gotten the other 2 yet. Sometimes I could feel my throat closing too (I'm guessing from the same allergies), and it's gotten better too. Now for these ringing ears! LOL.

I have been thinking about the placebo effect since it's been mentioned on other threads. Although I usually notice things after the fact LOL. LIke realizing that I don't have symptoms. Or changing something and it comes back, not realizing it had been gone. That's what happened with gluten and achy joints and heartburn for me - didn't notice it was gone until it came back. But the placebo effect then implies that the brain can heal - which is even more powerful to me.

I've been going back and reading a lot of old blog posts - the early mitochondrial series and some other posts. Interesting. From some of the things I read, I'm pretty sure I'm pre-diabetic. One thing that was mentioned was skin tags on neck - which Suzanne Somers book mentioned as well. Guess I'd better get that addressed.

I've also gone back and looked at some of my labs. Many things declined, but my blood DHEA and AM cortisol got better. This makes sense because I feel so much better during the day - it's crazy really. After I was told I had adrenal fatigue I felt kind of hopeless, especially reading what other people were doing and how long it was taking to fix. But within a month, I felt better. Almost like I didn't have adrenal fatigue at all. I couldn't believe it. That alone is enough to keep me here and wanting to continue to learn more. I've still got a lot to do, but man, working on my circadian cycles has been so HUGE to me. My moods are SO much better too. I think differently - which I would say means that either my dopamine or serotonin is getting better too. I need to do another ASI to see where I'm at (the last one was last March). But I have every confidence that it's better. And if it isn't, well, it doesn't reflect how I feel.

### diane — 2013-02-02T00:32:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-73715>

Odd day today.

Had my dr. appointment. Finally. Not my anti-aging doctor, my PCP. She's actually very good. I've been going to her for 20 years, and she's one that always tries to find the cause of an issue- not just treat the symptom. So talked to her a bit of what I've been doing - and specifically around minding the artificial light and timing of meals, etc. And she actually brought up the case of another patient who had bought a grounding sheet and how well it worked. I hadn't even talked about it - so the timing was pretty interesting. We talked a bit about EMF, and she does believe there's something to it. She was telling me how when she started (I'm guess 25 or so years ago) she only saw brain tumours in senior patients. Now she's diagnosed many in younger patients - so something is definitely at work. She's also very open when I ask her about things - testing, and I also got a prescription for Metformin. She uses it, not only for diabetics (and I have diabetes in my family - my dad and my cousin who is only a month younger than me) but to regulate cycle. And my cycle has been all over the place - so it wasn't a hard sell. Plus, I'm not the type to overdue prescriptions, so I'm sure that helps.

The other thing about going to the doctor is of course they weigh you, and take blood pressure etc. I was going to say no to weighing, then thought maybe I should since I hadn't weighed since the last time I was there, a year and a half ago. Damn. Up 20lbs. Really threw me for a loop. Not to say that I'm not aware that I've gained - but yes, the number bothered me. Crap. She asked me what I ate for breakfast - I lied, didn't want to tell her what I really ate. Then of course, the traditional thinking comes into play - maybe I shouldn't be eating as much fat. Maybe I should go for a run. Crap. It's really momentary, but damn, I have to put aside my half-assery and figure this out. I do feel better, that's for sure, and that counts for a lot.

And I think I need to get going on the BRHT. Got the request for testing today, along with the thyroid tests so we can see what the thyroid meds have been doing, but have to wait another couple of weeks to get those done. Patience grasshopper.

In the meantime, I start the Metformin tomorrow. Gonna really tighten up on the light and timing of dinner - it's been slipping a bit, and I don't turn the lights off until 7:30 or 8 (but feel an instant calm when I do, which is really interesting). Need to get in the tub more. I heard that loud and clear last night on the Q&A - get in the damn tub! And I need to plan out my BAB because if I don't, it also slips a bit, which messes things up for the rest of the day. Oh, and I'm starting to think that the butter in my tea is a problem, so cutting it out for a while to see how I do. I cut it this morning, and the green tea and coconut oil was still good (note - you have to blend it and it froths a bit).

I've also noticed lately pain on my leg when my laptop is sitting there. Is it psychosomatic? Don't know, but I have noticed it's where the battery is. Hmmmm.

Well, that's a lot for today. Now that I finally got the Vitamin D result, going to schedule a consult to get onto the next phase of getting better!

### caroline — 2013-02-02T00:59:31+0200

Your weight must be frustrating the crap out of you - wish I could be of some help. But it is great that you are feeling a lot better. Try really tightening up on the light stuff and maybe eating dinner earlier? I know if I have to turn the light on for something - even with my blueblockers on - my eyes really hurt

### diane — 2013-02-02T02:16:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-73744>

Yes Caroline - I totally agree regarding the light and dinner. Tonight I ate at about 6:30 - decided to walk the dogs before dinner. But what I was doing was coming home, we would all eat, then go for a walk. Made calamari with some onion, olive oil and lemon juice - not bad. But so disgusted that I felt kinda sick, so I didn't really eat much.

My vitamin D level is at 133 ("normal" is 75-200). Doctor says it's in normal range so isn't worried lol. I know it needs to be higher. But. The good news is that it was at 113 the last time, and that was taken in June. So I thin that a 133 in December is a great sign! So definitely some things positive. My DHEA was up and so was cortisol. So. Are these signs that my circadian levels are getting better - and going in the right direction means that other things will get fixed? That's my theory and I'm stickin' to it.

### diane — 2013-02-02T21:45:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-73833>

No Krista, haven't done 23 and me. I'm still trying to find my way around the "normal" labs - not sure what I would do with the results at this point. LOL.

Oh man. My cycle is changing again. This time, TOM came after 35 days. But it is really heavy - way heavier than I've ever had it. Even had to get up during the night because I could feel it. No pain, thank goodness. I'm hoping this means another shift is coming - but if it gets worse, not sure what I'll do. Is it possible the thyroid meds kicked it into a different gear? > Yes, the walk was amazing. Although I didn't throw myself on the ground to do some grounding LOL. Kinda forgot - we were having so much fun! They're going again tomorrow, so may join again. It's an hour drive each way, and I have to get up at 6:20 to go - but the dogs have so much fun that it's worth it! It was pretty icy today, so my older dog who had the 2 ACL surgeries was walking along the side trying to stay off the slippery parts as much as possible. Such a smart girl!

Too bad you can't make things happen and move to the country .... you and your dogs would just blossom....

### caroline — 2013-02-02T23:28:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-73848>

I always wanted to build my own log cabin .......

### diane — 2013-02-03T00:08:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-73856>

Caroline - my dad and his brothers have a log cabin on the north side of Algonquin park where they hunt and fish. No running water, no electricity. But they go every year. In October, my 78 year old dad went up with his 81 year old brother, 2 weeks after he had his 3rd stroke. Guess being in the wilderness runs in my blood! LOL.

Oh, I will be moving - just not sure if will be totally "country" or just less "urban" at this point. Funny enough my bank called today to remind me that my mortgage is up for renewal, so this may be my chance!

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-03T08:09:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-73902>

Moving is a big decision. Just remember that it will be stressful even when it goes smoothly so take that into account when you organise your supplements and self management.

### caroline — 2013-02-03T09:02:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-73915>

We moved from Canada to Australia - and I think when it is something you really want to do - it isn't really stressful at all - we couldn't wipe the grins off our faces .....When the right thing appears - you will know - everything will fall into place....

### SeaHorse — 2013-02-03T14:27:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-73919>

Caroline...I agree with you....I moved with my family after 25 years in Newfoundland and PEI back to the States...it was a huge process of closing down my horse business, selling our house, getting rid of more than half of what we owned..etc. etc. However, I was so looking forward to being back in the woods and mountains of Vermont that we just kind of powered through the year and half that it took to really move everything (five horses, dogs and cats, us, furniture, farm equipment etc.)

My big crash in health came afterwards....so I do think stress is part of the picture, yes, but I could have taken better care of myself. I thought I was, but I didn't have a clue compared to what I know now. SLEEP is number one. I think if you move or do anything big, you just really need to protect your sleep and then everything else will be manageable.

Think of the tools we have now to handle stress...amazing...

Also, Dianne, I know that any big change or pursuit of something new like a house 0r new place can actually release and increase a lot of Dopamine. It's the neurotransmitter involved in the seeking and searching impulse...hunting and gathering, shopping, mating....

### diane — 2013-02-03T15:37:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-73928>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=73919)
>
> Also, Dianne, I know that any big change or pursuit of something new like a house 0r new place can actually release and increase a lot of Dopamine. It's the neurotransmitter involved in the seeking and searching impulse...hunting and gathering, shopping, mating....

Well darn - is that why I love to shop so much! I spend loads of time looking at things and researching. Maybe from the low dopamine! LOL.

### diane — 2013-02-03T16:00:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-73938>

I started reading The Hormone Solution by Thierry Hertoghe (thanks Kathylu for the reco!). Then going to read Sex, Lies and Menopause that Darleen recommends.

It's clear from reading only a few chapters in the book that I have had thyroid problems for years, probably progesterone too. And that pretty much all the women in my mom's family had progesterone issues.

My doctor started me at 30mg of the thyroid meds, and wants to test after 4 weeks and then go up. I think that's going to be too slow to be honest. In the book he recommends going up every 2 weeks. And I can tell after a week that it hasn't made much difference - so I think we have to push it. Only thing is, I only have enough to get me to my next appoinment, so if I start increasing, I'll be out. So I either call and tell them what I'm doing (hahaha - oh so unlike me). Or just stick to the 30mg and talk to her in my next appointment. However, my thinking right now is that I don't have enough time and patience to wait.

She had also mentioned progesterone cream the last time I was there, but I decided to start the thyroid on its own first. I'm still happy with that decision (current TOM aside) so I can see the impact of each thing separately. But my next appointment I'm going to push for progesterone as I think it's a big part of my problem too. I'm not expecting that doing these things will cause weight to magically drop off - but I kinda feel like I'm pushing a boulder up a steep hill if I don't get these things going.

Both of my doctors, in response to me telling them about changes in my TOM, that it's normal given my age. But I don't think it has to be normal and that I can't try to make it better. And while I think changes are happening due to age, I also think that some of the more recent ones are shifts based on the things I've been doing, because the timing has correlated to these changes. I used the analogy of the body as a control panel before - so I've turned some dials, and now some other switches need attention me thinks!

Also have had 1 full day of Metformin. Of course you can't tell anything from a day, but have not had nausea. And after I ate at 6:00 last night, no night time hunger or cravings. Although my stomach was growling when I woke up this morning.

Actually, one thing I did notice this morning. I woke up at around 6:20, more fully awake then normal. Realize that this is the time I had been getting up to take my thyroid med during the week, then going back to bed for the 30 minutes cause I was still tired. Have also noticed being warm in the morning when I wake up. Not hot and sweaty - warm. So maybe something is starting to work a bit afterall.

### SeaHorse — 2013-02-03T17:06:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-73949>

I had the same issue about running out of meds because my doctor wanted to wait six months before seeing me again! What I did was go through the prescription and then order some Thyroidgold online. So I've been dosing myself upwards and will test again in the next week or so. THEN go back to the Dr. and say.."this is what I need" Waiting weeks and weeks between increasing is not recommended by anyone in the know. I did overshoot it and get some A-fib going in the fall, but it's gone now...I'm actually feeling like I need to increase the dose again to get my temp. up. I read a good quote by Albert Schweitzer today: "Each patient carries his own doctor inside him."

I've never even been to Algonquin and I had two chances to go...but back in the day I was a 'wuss' and did not want to go b/c of rain. WHAT WAS I THINKING!? O well.

### diane — 2013-02-03T20:20:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-learning-by-diane.5093/post-73978>

Next time you get a chance to go to Algonquin, leap on it!

Or just tell them straight up I need to increase. I am not exactly sure what it is honestly - but could ask the compounding pharmacy. Drugs aren't easy to get online here, as I'm sure you know.

### caroline — 2013-02-04T00:26:17+0200
