**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magic-live-music.5096/post-66832>

I am sitting here reading the forum before going to bed.. and then I hear music from downstairs. My mom plays piano and my brothers daughter that is visiting plays violin.. Christmas music. It is so beautiful.. something happends in my heart I can feel it fysically!

It is so sweet.. like sweet numbness but in a amazing way.. wow. What is this? I had some huge stress today and I guess my heart was still too tired but this music did wonder? Just very simple home made live music nothing fancy but how good it felt. I need to start playing and listening to more live music. I am now sure it must be very healing!

### prAna303 — 2012-12-08T16:40:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magic-live-music.5096/post-66838>

I think itÂ´s quite simple. When the filter of junk in our modern world begin to clear up we start to resonate with things. My thought is that everything in this world is about somekind of electricity. When we eat junk, drink junk, sleep junk, consume junk and everything is "dirty" we cannot se the special things in classic and live music, we think it is boring. We seek more and more volume and punchlines. When the fog clears up that junksciencestuff become very disturbing and the small things become more enjoyable. We really enjoy it because we se the details. This applies to everything. Even the smallest change in detail resonate with our ownÂ frequency.

I did not know.. Oh man it was so beautiful I just stopped right there.. sat down at the stairs and enjoyed the music so immensely. I all of a sudden was in a totally different word.
Like heaven.

I get paralyzed... that is.

### SeaHorse — 2012-12-08T20:30:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magic-live-music.5096/post-66888>

Resonate is the word isn't it? Â Emotions and music are so closely linked through resonance. We feel the vibrations and they take us (or create, or remind us) of our feelings. ### Inger — 2012-12-08T16:27:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-candles.5098/post-66836>

I recognized it is way too expensive for me to use candles all the evening as it gets dark already at 3 PM here now... so I started to look out for some other options. What I found was the small 15 to 18 watt lamps that are ment for the oven or the fridge. They work perfectly well in a small lamp covered with a lamp-shade! I am just happy to have found these because they are cheap so much cheaper then candles. I guess a red light bulb would be even better but they did not have those in the shop here, I will look after them in Helsinki city one day I am there.
Now.. perfect would be to have it totally dark..lol.. but I need to get something done still.. but maybe next winter. Step by step.

### granitereno@gmail.com — 2012-12-11T13:16:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-candles.5098/post-67119>

I have been making my own oil lamps for a few weeks.Â  (I started while preparing for Sandy...)

I use a hempÂ "wick" through a cork (covered in tin foil- I didn't use all of it for hats!)

I float this in canola oil (mixed with a little bit of pepermint oil) in a martini glass.

It looks really nice ...and smells great.

### Inger — 2012-12-28T14:28:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-candles.5098/post-68599>

Here are my brothers (and I am taking the picture) making olive oil lamps as in the video above! It went great! ### SeaHorse — 2012-12-28T14:56:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-candles.5098/post-68603>

I was wondering if regular oil lamps would burn with olive oil? I have a number of old lamps and I've been using store bought paraffin Â which doesn't smell at all but is a petroleum product..I'd like to use something a little less toxic. I'm going to experiment and hopefully not ruin a lamp!

I love reading by our woodstove with the lamp light..it doesn't happen every night and the past week or so with Christmas things were a little bright, but at least my family is accepting the dark this winter. I was the one who had the really bad light habits...Because I had S.A.D. I always just thought more light was better so I used my SAD light all the time, even at night. No wonder my sleep disappeared. Now I can count on a great sleep almost every night and I don't feel depressed in the darkness...I'm enjoying it for the first time in a very very long time.

I love your picture Ingar..I recently made some beeswax candles and ornaments. The beeswax smells so good and I was lucky to find a bucket of it in our barn that was left by the previous owners. I had some old teacups that I glued to their saucers and then melted beeswax in the teacup with a wick. It's a pretty candle and easy to refill the cup when the wax is gone.

### caroline — 2012-12-08T17:37:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-66852>

I want to start LHT but have never done anything like this before. Â Kettlebells seem like fun and something I would like to do. Â From what I read I should start pretty light but will need a heavier one soon after - so I should buy a pair? Â I am not sure what weight to start at tho Â 4Kg or 6Kg or 8Kg?

I am a real novice \[and older\] with not much strength - still some muscle weakness but getting better Â  - but I have/want to start somewhere. Â 

Also I see there are pro ones and just regular ones. Â The regular ones have much bigger handles. It seems like the pro ones are better for a variety of reasons Â - is that correct?

thanks for any help.

BTW - my goal is to become an older version of Inger ...lol Â .......I know, I know ...good luck with that! Â  Â JK says we have to have big dreams! Â That is so true. Â I have always overstretched my limits - it was always intuitive .......to be more - I had to want more.

### diane — 2012-12-08T18:13:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-66857>

There was a book recommended to me to learn how to kettle bell. It's called Swing, and the author is Tracy Reifkins. I got it on Amazon as a kindle download.

Â 

It's great because it shows you proper technique, but then gives beginner programs all the way to advanced. To start she recommends only 10-15 minutes a day. And start with a light weight and get heavier as you get better.Â 

Â 

I've done them before so I have a 20 lb to start. We have a store here that you can sell used sporting goods - if you have one like that, would be good to start light and then trade up. It's not like you can wear out kettlebells. LOL.

### caroline — 2012-12-08T18:38:17+0200

### pthomaslandry@gmail.com — 2012-12-09T20:24:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-66963>

Most women start with an 8kg, but a 12 and a 16 are a good idea, as you will likely outgrow the 8kg fairly quickly.

Just ensure that you move from your hip joints (bottom of pelvis), and not from your back (top of pelvis) and you should be good to go.

Too many people blame kettlebells and not form when they hurt themselves.

I'd reccomend spending an hour or two with a certified trainer if you have any doubts.

### fitness@home — 2012-12-09T21:41:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-66967>

I liked the DVDs from Skogg for learning proper KB technique:

http://www.skoggsystem.com/

KBs are my favorite form of exercise. I like that it can marry weights, range of motion, and endurance type exercise together.

Need to get back to this soon. Anyone up for 200 swings per day challenge? Easy to work up to and doesn't take much time to complete. Can be done in short sets of 25-50 swings each.

### diane — 2012-12-10T19:25:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-67055>

I tried to find a certified trainer - it was so difficult. And I live in a large city! Hence the book, which really does explain form in great detail. The author is RKC certified I believe, so it was the best option for me.

### furface — 2012-12-10T20:29:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-67061>

I'd also suggest you try Craigslist or a similar place for some used kettlebells. They are quite expensive when purchasing new, and they don't really wear out, so perhaps you can find a good deal on some used ones from someone who doesn't want theirs any more.

I've used kettlebells myself at the gym, but at home I use adjustable dumbbells in place of kettlebells. I either use them with one hand around the grip, or with two hands around one of the plates. Works basically the same, and I can adjust the weight to however heavy I want. I have 3 sets of spin lock collar dumbbell handles with nearly 200 lbs. of plates from 1 1/4 lbs., 2 1/2 lbs, 5 lbs, 7 1/2 lbs, 10 lbs, and 25 lbs.

### caroline — 2014-02-11T23:31:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-109698>

I love my kettlebells. I think I have already said somewhere. They are great for core strength. I noticed huge improvements almost right away and only did about 35 swings a few times a week.
I started with 8kg. I credit them with my body appearing - that I didn't know I had ....

### sooperb — 2014-02-13T12:21:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-109966>

As an expert on buying then not using exercise equipment I also bought a kettle bell. I made a big mistake, it was 8kg and I could barely lift it so I had to buy a lighter one - 4 kg. even that was a challenge. I have made great use of them since, as bedroom doorstops  I bought a DVD from eBay which turned out to be a copy so was missing some of the instructions. Long story short, the rights owner ended up sending me a bona fide copy by which time my enthusiasm had waned again.

Funnily enough I was wondering if kbs would fit the LHT and was just reading about how even doing as little as the initial warm up swing followed by a lift is really good. I've also been practising squat sitting, it's a lot of fun lol.

### Da-mo — 2018-02-04T11:16:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-234097>

Do some yard work. You could swing an axe and chop firewood, then stack it instead of kettlebells. I have 5 acres that needs lots of stuff done if anyone is looking for a place to train

But seriously, you can get enough exercise just doing stuff that needs doing without going out of your way to find a workout. I once lived on a hill so steep that I used to tie a rope to the lawnmower and lower it down the slope then pull it back up again. . . . . with a catcher on. Also, I would ride a bicycle to work instead of driving.

It cracks me up seeing people who drive to the gym then spend 20 minutes on the treadmill when they could have just walked there in the open air and sunshine.

# Thread: I guess I am allergic to sea food or it could be something else.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-i-am-allergic-to-sea-food-or-it-could-be-something-else.5100/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### x_danny_x — 2012-12-08T18:56:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-i-am-allergic-to-sea-food-or-it-could-be-something-else.5100/post-66867>

well, it has been one hell of a battle to figure out what is wrong with me.
Right now I am in the path of healing since I no longer use nose medicine so i can breath and can breath well enough now that I can sleep comfortably without using the Afrin nose spray or another nose spray.

i am attributing this healing to CTing ofcourse and also starting to eat sea food.

i never tried or bother to eat sea food before finding about Doctor Kruse and his data about sea food.

my stomach just cant handle it. I get diarrhea. I have no problem eating other type of foods. i wonder if i just need to give it time and take it slow or since i have never started to eat sea food before.

anybody else experience this?

### freshveggies — 2012-12-08T18:59:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-i-am-allergic-to-sea-food-or-it-could-be-something-else.5100/post-66870>

could be re-ingineering your gut.Â  Same thing happened when I ate the tails of shrimp and a couple times after. Â  Now it doesn't happen when I eat them.Â  Dr. Kruse mentioned that this could happen in a webinar.

### x_danny_x — 2012-12-08T19:12:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-i-am-allergic-to-sea-food-or-it-could-be-something-else.5100/post-66872>

re-engineering in order to digest sea food or is it healing the gut???

Â 

i have a whole bunch of fish fillets and i constantly eat oysters.Â  well i limit them for like once a day since they are kind of expensive.

Â 

i actually able to withstand them better when i chose to eat sea food raw, without even being cooked at all.

### freshveggies — 2012-12-08T20:00:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-i-am-allergic-to-sea-food-or-it-could-be-something-else.5100/post-66878>

From what I know which is limited---gut healing.Â

### caroline — 2012-12-08T20:19:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-i-am-allergic-to-sea-food-or-it-could-be-something-else.5100/post-66884>

I have been eating raw oysters, salmon etc and raw meat for sometime now but the other day I had raw oysters and got very sick. Huge bloating - my stomach was so sore I had to take my clothes off and very bad diarrhea that lasted for about 10 hours. So I am guessing something was the matter with the oysters alltho they tasted/smelled fine and I bought them from a huge and very busy fish market. So now I am scared to have them again - I don't know how I would tell if they are okay or not. Any ideas?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-08T20:35:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-i-am-allergic-to-sea-food-or-it-could-be-something-else.5100/post-66889>

I would not be scared.........we are designed to eat them. Â When you fall you get back and repeat and re evaluate. Â If youre wrong it could undercut your long term success.

### caroline — 2012-12-08T20:44:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-i-am-allergic-to-sea-food-or-it-could-be-something-else.5100/post-66891>

I would not be scared.........we are designed to eat them. Â When you fall you get back and repeat and re evaluate. Â If youre wrong it could undercut your long term success.

you always know how to cut to the chase - thanks Why can't I do that for myself????

### x_danny_x — 2012-12-08T20:54:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-i-am-allergic-to-sea-food-or-it-could-be-something-else.5100/post-66895>

caroline,Â  it seems to me that it is the bad oyster.Â  even though it looked good,Â  something was bad about it since you didnt have issues with it from eating it before or any other sea food.

for me,Â  no matter what the sea food or where i buy it from,Â  it gives me diarrhea. Â  so i know it is me and not the sea food.

Â

im also wondering if my body is absorbing all the nutrients when ingesting sea food when having diarrhea?Â Â  my guess that it is not. i think im only absorbing partial and not all the nutrients that come with the sea food.

### caroline — 2012-12-08T23:40:13+0200

Danny - I have some more thoughts! Â I was just down at the beach swimming in the ocean and I was thinking about you. Â Are you close to a lake/ocean? Â Maybe you could swim with the fish and take them on by osmosis or something LOL.

Anyway - what I was thinking - lately I seem to be a lot more sensitive to stuff. Â I have already mentioned the cans. ### Inger — 2012-12-09T07:04:21+0200

Maybe it is table salt and not real seasalt. IDK but sometimes it makes me mad that they have to do something weird with everything to make it look better, keep better and who knows what. Too sad. There is still a lot of great stuff out there to eat. About allergies for seafood. I read somewhere that some people are allergic against certains stuff they add to almost all seafood today. It was phosphates I think? I am not 100% sure but I think it was that. It makes the seafood keep better and hold the water better so it looks better after defrosting too. I guess it could make people swell too..lol

### Linz — 2012-12-10T06:39:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-i-am-allergic-to-sea-food-or-it-could-be-something-else.5100/post-66994>

Raw shrimp is usually treated with sodium bisulphite as soon as it is caught to prevent black spot. There is a bit more information at livestrong.com but sulphite preservatives are declared on packaging in many countries as they do have some health risks.Â

### MartiD — 2012-12-10T11:23:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-i-am-allergic-to-sea-food-or-it-could-be-something-else.5100/post-67005>

My Mother was deadly allergic to scallops. She was fine with all other seafood but had to be careful in restaurants for cross contamination. She would breakout in hives, throat would swell, etc. I have avoided eating scallops until about 6 months ago but didn't have a reaction. They are one of my favorites now.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-fat-juice-left-in-the-pan-after-browning-grass-fed-ground-beef-good-to-consume.5101/post-66883>

I realize that we're supposed to consume a lot of healthy fats, esp on Leptin Reset, which I am trying to do...coconut oil, bacon fat, fats from fish, etc, but I was just wondering is the fat/grease/juice that's left in the pan after you cook, or brown grass-fed ground beef, good to consume as well?

Â 

Maybe a better question would be, what are the fats that are NOT safe/good to consume...if any? Esp if trying to lose bodyfat.

Â 

Thanks.

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-08T22:32:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-fat-juice-left-in-the-pan-after-browning-grass-fed-ground-beef-good-to-consume.5101/post-66904>

> I realize that we're supposed to consume a lot of healthy fats, esp on Leptin Reset, which I am trying to do...coconut oil, bacon fat, fats from fish, etc, but I was just wondering is the fat/grease/juice that's left in the pan after you cook, or brown grass-fed ground beef, good to consume as well?
>
> Â 
>
> Maybe a better question would be, what are the fats that are NOT safe/good to consume...if any? Esp if trying to lose bodyfat.
>
> Â 
>
> Thanks.

Oh yeah that's good fat. Good veg fats are olive, avocado, coconut. All the animal and fish fats are good. bad fats are industrial seed oils like canola, peanut, soy, cottonseed, sunflower If the label says vegetable oil-pass. I render my own lard from pork fat and tallow from beef fat. CO is particularly good for weight loss as your body won't store it.

# Thread: Want to understand the power of temperature for human biology?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-understand-the-power-of-temperature-for-human-biology.5102/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-08T20:27:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-understand-the-power-of-temperature-for-human-biology.5102/post-66885>

Read this and think about what I have laid out in the CT series..........this is quite important to read before you listen to the December Webinar.

Â 

In an ingenious experiment, M . Mendelssohn (cited by Jenning 1906) observed that paramecia randomly dispersed themselves when ambient temperature was kept around 20 C. However, when a temperature gradient was established in their medium from 26 to 38 C or from 10 to 25 C, they congregated close to the area maintained between 24 and 28 degrees C. Moreover, when the temperature of their medium was elevated beyond their preferred temperature, these organisms became more active,Â performing a series of rotatory movements projecting them toward the cooler region . Once they reached a cooler , more comfortable environment , they resumed random locomotion . A similar , abeit slower , type of " avoiding locomotion " occurs when paramecia move from a cool to a hot environment.

Jenning (1906) performed a series of experiments in paramecia that led him to conclusions similar to those of Mendelssohn . Through the microscope , Jenning observed an activation of paramecia movements as the temperature of their medium was uniformly elevated between 40 and 45 degrees C. **When a drop of cold water was placed in their environment , **paramecia gathered in this region** , avoiding the heated area. These experiments clearly show that unicellular organisms without brains have the capacity to select a preferred temperature behaviorally, and therefore to protect themselves from environmental extremes. Â (Implies cold is primordial to all life forms)

Thus , environmental heat was a source of deleterious stimulation " but it was also an essential source of energy. Living organisms required heat for chemical reactions . Although some of the heat for these reactions came from metabolic activity , the main source of heat energy came fromÂ the environment. Therefore, _the control of environmental temperature had to be coupled to the production of internal heat._** It is therefore plausible to infer that unicellular organisms had some rudimentary capacity to control their internal temperature.

"Excerpted from the book called Hot Brain"

COUNTERINTUITIVE MOTIVATION POINT: Cold draws life to it..............this is true in all mammal life forms even when they are not aware of it. Â Mammals exploded after K-T........cold once again was what the environment became overnight after the asteroid hit. Â To maintain internal warmth they had to burn fat efficiently to survive. Â No carbs were around to grow because light cycles were disrupted and temperature fell like a brick. Â All the food was in the deep cold polar seas. Â Connecting any dots people? Â Our deep ancestors and us are still tied together............by one thing. Â EPIGENETICS.

Â 

Then read this gem of a blog from Grace at Dr. B.G blog: Â 

Â 

http://drbganimalpharm.blogspot.com/2012/11/mitochondria-fuel-fluxxx-and-heat-nsfw.html

Â 

After reading her blog it is clear people are beginning to realize the implications of cold temperatures and energy efficiency or inefficiency and what a big brain actually means for biochemistry and life in general for wellness or for illness........The tide is turning because people are starting to question many parts of paleo dogma. Â That is a good thing. Â I hope when you read this post before the December webinar is confuses you...........then re read it right after you hear the December webinar for the first time..........and do it before the Q & A.........and I bet your mind will be opened wide to a new reality about all mammals and especially about modern human women and the challenges they face.......and why they might face it by design............moreover because they are built this way, what might the implications be when things go awry in circadian signaling for any reason...........you might understand your plight better if you have a vagina and breasts or love someone who has that type of equipment. Â 

Â 

When we all know better, we tend to do better.........EPIGENETICS/WOMEN/FACTOR X.........Trifecta.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-08T22:23:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-understand-the-power-of-temperature-for-human-biology.5102/post-66903>

We have a sweet spot for optimal function. The question is do we want to get there or remain comfortable? I think I've discovered that my sweet spot in CT is below 44 deg I recover faster & warm up quicker. That tells me my mitochondria prefer deep cold to moderate cold. Why I think they work better there b/c they don't have to work as hard it's effortless for them at those temps. Where as CT when the water is just a few deg warmer it's harder which tells me they're not as efficient at 50 deg. So if they're not efficient there they're down right sluggish in summer when it's hot So this leads me to question "Are women designed to function better at lower temps?" "Are we designed to leverage cold to influence our progeny?" I ask this b/c up until doing the LR the only other time I've ever been HOT like I am now is in pregnancy and all I wanted to do was lay down in the snow and revel in it but didn't for the fear I couldn't get up out or close to 3 ft of snow. What were my mitochondria doing? What are they doing now?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-12-10T21:40:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-genes-interaction.5106/post-67073>

If rats can do it, I'm guessing we can too....

Fetal fuels. I. Utilization of ketones by isolated tissues at various stages of maturation and maternal nutrition during late gestation

Metabolism, Volume 26, Issue 6, June 1977

Abstract
The availability and utilization of B-hydroxybutyrate as an alternate oxidative fuel during fasting hypoglycemia has been examined in the rat conceptus at 18 and 20 days gestation. A 48-hr maternal fast between days 16 and 18 or 18 and 20 resulted in a 50% fall in fetal glucose levels and a marked rise in B-hydroxybutyrate, i.e., 30-fold at 18 and 60-fold at 20 days. Tissue concentrations of B-hydroxybutyrate or acetoacetate did not exceed extracellular levels. Placenta, fetal brain, carcass, and liver all oxidized 14C-labeled B-hydroxybutyrate to 14CO2 when incubated in vitro in the presence of B-hydroxybutyrate. Highest rates of oxidation were apparent in the placenta, followed by brain, liver, and carcass. The D isomer of B-hydroxybutyrate appeared to be oxidized preferentially by all tissues studied. Despite levels of 3-ketoacid CoA transferase and acetoacetyl CoA thiolase lower at 18 than at 20 days, rates of oxidation in individual tissues incubated under identical concentrations of substrate were similar at both times. In liver and brain, increasing rates of 14CO2 generation proportionate to graded concentrations of B-hydroxybutyrate in vitro indicated that such rates were probably determined by substrate availability. B-hydroxybutyrate oxidation in extrahepatic fetal tissues was unaffected by maternal fasting. By contrast, fetal liver derived from fasted mothers generated significantly less 14CO2 from B-hydroxybutyrate that livers from fed mothers. It has been suggested that capabilities for ketone utilization are widespread in tissues of the conceptus, and that such utilization may fulfill in part the oxidative demands for continued anabolic growth during fasting hypoglycemia in the mother.

### ruby — 2012-12-10T22:04:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-genes-interaction.5106/post-67079>

Thanks, KiwiLauren. I wonder if this is also one of mechanisms that plays a role here (this is an extreme clear cut example but the mechanism could be similar in the Western world in cases of less severe nutritional deficiencies):
"Programmed for Diabetes - Researchers are finding that underfed mothers produce small, undernourished babies with metabolisms equipped for deprivation and unable to cope with plenty. As a child growing up in an Indian village near the Arabian Sea, at least once a week Sonar plodded for two hours along a dirt path to collect water for her eight-member family, which existed on two meals a day of vegetables and rice. David Barker, a British doctor from the University of Southampton whose research has helped explain the origins of so-called lifestyle ailments says malnutrition during a babyâ€™s development affects how a personâ€™s body behaves for a lifetime. An undernourished fetus prioritizes sugar for its growing brain. To make more glucose available in the blood, the fetus stores less of the energy in its muscles by making the muscles resistant to the effects of insulin. What starts as a clever survival trick in the womb becomes a liability in later life. When food is freely available but the muscles canâ€™t store excess glucose, the blood floods with sugar and diabetes develops. Too much sugar in the blood damages the heart, small blood vessels and nerves, compounding the risk of heart attack, stroke and kidney failure. â€œThe biggest recipe for chronic disease is to have a very poor start in life and then to be in a situation where youâ€™re rapidly transitioning towards excess nutrition and inadequate activity,â€ says Caroline Fall, 56, a professor of international pediatric epidemiology at Southampton, who joined Barkerâ€™s group in 1990 and has studied diseases in India since 1991. â€œThatâ€™s absolutely characteristic of whatâ€™s happening in India.â€
Chittaranjan S. Yajnik observed that Indian newborns were typically small and thin -- like their mothers -- yet carried proportionally MORE insulin-repelling FAT than infants in England. He found low birth weight predicted insulin resistance in Indian children, identifying those already on the road to diabetes and cardiovascular disease, just as Barker had posited."

# Thread: What amazing changes are you noticing in your life?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-amazing-changes-are-you-noticing-in-your-life.5107/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2012-12-09T01:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-amazing-changes-are-you-noticing-in-your-life.5107/post-66912>

I know there used to be a thread on what changes we are noticing but I thought maybe it was time for a new one.
Jackkruse.com is almost a year old in a couple of months. I thought it would be great to think about all the things we have accomplished courtesy of our own hard work and Dr. Kruse and all the amazing people on this forum and..... Dr. Kruse did say to put pen to paper so to speak!
When I read the posts by new people it brings to mind all the questions I had - so many things I wasn't sure of - even tho it is all here. I seemed to always need to circle back and pick up another piece of the puzzle - always because of something that some one else or JK said. Sometimes he nudges gently and some times he is like a freight train!
It has been an amazing journey that I wish I had taken years ago but none the less -I am here now. When I think about that in particular I realize that this journey only started for Dr. K himself 7 or 8 years ago.
I wanted to talk about changes that I notice - but - you know what - there are way to many. An example of this - I had been seeing a grief counselor for a couple of years. I hadn't seen her for over three months because of travel and life etc. until the other day. Well -she couldn't believe her eyes \[and ears\] There is a 360 degree change in me and we spent my whole hour talking about Jack Kruse!!! As usual for me lately - I couldn't sit still, couldn't stop with the silly grin and just this amazing sense of joy.
I think the most important thing that I have learned is to think ...think for myself and think about myself and others and listen to my body's cues. Epipaleo makes the signalling clearer. I am sure my wiring was pretty screwy/non existent before - that is a technical term by the way!
I would love to hear about the changes you are noticing and how it is affecting friends and family - the people you love most in your life..... and I think it would be great for Dr. Kruse to know that we are listening - even tho sometimes it must seem like we are not. He keeps doing the heavy lifting - so we need to as well - it seems only fair- that is if we want an exciting, dynamic life full of love and endless possibilities - in whatever form that takes. I have so much respect for everyone on this forum. We are all so different and contribute in many different ways - you never really know when something you say is going to have a positive affect on someone else and give them a missing piece to their puzzle. That happens for me over and over and over again. sometimes it takes days or weeks to sink in. LOL
I seems that this has turned out to be kind of an open love letter to all of us. I hope that doesn't sound gross!

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-09T10:14:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-amazing-changes-are-you-noticing-in-your-life.5107/post-66926>

I love this!

I've noticed:

\--I don't get calluses on my feet anymore.Â  They stay smooth for months now without pedicures!Â  Big one for me, as I actually own one of those skin-shaver things (not just the PedEgg, but the actual razor blade one) because I'd have to shave my callusesÂ weekly.Â  Always blamed it on my shoes, duh, now I know it was my diet.

\--I had a skin issue "down there" for decades.Â  I was given every test by every OB/GYN, including biopsies; no one could figure out the cause.Â  I was even sent to Baylor College of Medicine so that a Professor Emeritus could examine me.Â  No cause.Â  It was terrible and caused horrendous itching, rash, and also large white thick scaly patches.Â  The diagnosis bounced between pre-cancerous lesions to lichen sclerosis to just plain old eczema.Â  Maddening.Â  Yes, you guessed it....my skin has been completely clear "down there" for about a year.

\--I found me a new doc!Â  Yay!Â  Found her from paleophysicians.com, and she asked me if I'm eating "enough" fat!Â  LOL.Â  Got the complete Genova nutritional panel, and she has me on Vit. D and B's and curcumin, as well as other things.Â  It's like, OMG, a doctor who didn't just hand me a bunch of Rx's for nonsense drugs...what a concept.

\--Biggie:Â  Sleep.Â  I actually sleep straight through for 7-8 hours a night.Â  Vivid dreams.Â 

\--Not afraid of a 50 degree tub anymore!Â  I was a holdout.Â  When Dr. K. first started writing about CT, I thought ok, here's where I step off.Â  But one boring Saturday, I just....did it.Â  And look forward to my tub time!

# Thread: What's the best way to read the blog?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-best-way-to-read-the-blog.5108/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area

### Ruthie — 2012-12-09T12:40:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-best-way-to-read-the-blog.5108/post-66938>

Hi there,

Â 

Any pointers for how to go about reading Dr. Kruse's information on the blog? Â I am just starting in December 2012, and feeling overwhelmed and unsure of how to methodically get through all the information. Â By date? Â Index? (I looked at the index, but each category didn't seem to be in any particular order.) Â 

Â 

Thanks,

Ruthie

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-09T13:14:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-best-way-to-read-the-blog.5108/post-66940>

Hi Ruthie, and welcome.Â  Hey, the blogs are still overwhelming for those of us who have been here since last year at this time, so don't get too worried about it.Â  Thing is, you will read a blog about 3 times and still not fully understand it; that's ok.Â  It will start to make sense as you read different blogs.Â  You'll be like....oh....and light bulbs will start going off.

I'd say the best way to start is chronologically.Â  Skim through a blog post and read through the comments.Â  Skim through the next one, comments, and so on.Â  Then, when something occurs to you that you've maybe already heard before, go back to those first ones.Â  It's a lot like taking a test for a subject you just don't understand.Â  Also, read the Levees in the Quilt.Â  Don't worry if you don't understand them now, just sort of read through them, because you'll see references to some Levees in different places.

I've read every blog post multiple times, and I still go back.Â  I will go by index; for example, a few weeks ago, I wanted to re-read on Sleep, so I re-read all the sleep blogs.

And if you have questions on a particular blog after you've read through it and still don't understand it, and it hasn't already been asked in the comments section, feel free to post a question either here on the forums or on the blog itself.

### diane — 2012-12-09T16:39:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-best-way-to-read-the-blog.5108/post-66945>

I agree with Pam - it does help to re-read the blogs a few times. And they do get easier to read.Â 

Â 

You may want to start with the Leptin Guide (I think there's a page that organizes them), then to CT and the BG series. The Hormone 101 blog is very important too.Â 

Â 

Don't try to rush through them- but if I got stuck and didn't understand something, I did keep moving then came back later. Sometimes it takes reading something else to help the pieces fall into place!

### caroline — 2012-12-09T17:26:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-best-way-to-read-the-blog.5108/post-66950>

Great suggestions! Â I still struggle Â - I think because I will be reading and something I don't understand will come up so I will have to look that up and it becomes an endless circle. Â It does help to focus on one subject. Â I find tho - when you go back and re read -some things all of a sudden stand out or you missed them completely the first or second time around. Â We just keep trying! Â  Â Hopefully this is helping us to make new brain cells! Â A friend said to me the other day - you really have a handle on all this stuff and I thought OMG - if you only knew. Â fake it till you make it I guess. Â  Â  Â  Â A year from now..... you won't recognise Â YOU .....and you will be so proud.

### Ruthie — 2012-12-10T21:03:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-the-best-way-to-read-the-blog.5108/post-67066>

Thanks for the suggestions! Â Looks like we all have our work cut our for us!

Cheers,

# Thread: Started gaining weight back at 4 months
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-gaining-weight-back-at-4-months.5110/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Claudia — 2012-12-09T15:27:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-gaining-weight-back-at-4-months.5110/post-66942>

HI, everyone, I've been off the forum for awhile as I had a lot of personal life problems to attend to and had to take a computer break. My last hcg round ended beginning of May and I lost the 8 lbs I wanted to goal. I had a good summer maintaining on p3 Paleo, stayed at ldw and even a little below. Then, in September I started gaining around the time when it becomes fall but I can't say for sure. I know I started feeling thyroid symptoms coming back. I have very low t3, almost out of range at 2.6 (the range starts at 2.5) and my tsh is 1.5, t4 is in the middle, and rt3 is very high at 29 (range ends at 35). I have a conversion issue but I haven't arrived at the reason for it. So, with lots of hypothyroid symptoms for years, I was given Cytomel (t3) and started that in the summer at a very low dose of 5mcg. In the fall, I called the doc and said I didn't feel well (major symptom is fatigue) and he increased my Cytomel to 10 mcg. It seemed a little better but not great...not anywhere near how I felt in the summer, which was increased energy and more myself. ANd the 10mcg didn't stop the creeping weight gain. Then, another doc prescribed progesterone cream (200mg) compounded and I started that and gained another 5 lbs in a week and had awful symptoms from it (couldn't function, awful brain fog, nauseated) and think it was way too high of a dose. I can't fit in my clothes, bloated, lots of fatigue despite the 10mcg of cytomel. I don't know what to do about the progesterone cream as I certainly don't need to keep gaining weight. I think I'm going to stop taking it, it's just frightening to be gaining weight that I can't control.

I'm thinking of starting a round right after Christmas. I just feel so frustrated because I was done. I think something is wrong with me with this hormonal gain. This is not the first time this happened. I did 2 round in 2011, lost to goal, and gained it back, not because of eating but because of thyroid or some other Â hormonal imbalance. The docs I see are ok but not excellent, they haven't prevented my weight gain and they don't get a the root of what's wrong. So, I'm just wondering if anyone else out there has experienced this over and over, lose with hcg, gain it back. It's the only way I can lose any weight but it's also a lot slower than others lose.

### MsYo — 2012-12-11T14:45:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-gaining-weight-back-at-4-months.5110/post-67127>

How are your vitamin D levels? Is the progesterone biodentical? Are you eating wheat/gluten/dairy?? Sounds like inflammation? Maybe labs posted with ranges will help with generating suggestions.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-12T10:56:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-gaining-weight-back-at-4-months.5110/post-67207>

Likely a mitochondrial inefficiency that manifests via a bad hormone panel.

### Claudia — 2012-12-17T22:28:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-gaining-weight-back-at-4-months.5110/post-67773>

Thanks for the replies.

Â 

My free t3 is 2.6 (range 2.5-4.3 pg/mL)

tsh 1.350 (0.270-4.200 uIU/mL)

free t4 Â 1.15 ng/dL (0.8301.62 ng/dL0

Â 

I feel lousy. I don't know if it's a good idea for me to try another hcg round or not. Don't know if it's actually making me worse off in the long run.

I'm going to look this up Jack:Â a mitochondrial inefficiency that manifests via a bad hormone panel.

Thanks. I also recently found out I have the gene mutation MTHFR c677T, and I am Â homozygous with 2 copies. ### Shijin13 — 2013-05-04T16:01:58+0300

> [AlaskaLadyie said:](/goto/post?id=85406)
>
> **Jack...... could we get somesort of explaination to what this means.... please**

When your mitochondria aren't functioning properly, it directly impacts your hormone panel. think of if this way: your car isn't running well, and you need to take it in for a tune up but don't/can't for what ever reason. your car continues to run badly, you spend more $ on gas b/c its not efficient using the fuel you give it.

we were designed to run on fat, but we've been told that's wrong for the past 30-40years and we've avoided it. so now we're trying to teach our body how to use fat for fuel and stop using glucose. when you get into using FAT as your fuel you can enter the PPP if you've got your hormones right fixed. when you enter the PPP you are using the ATP you make from the fat to allow your body to use water as energy instead of ATP... as you move toward this you begin to fix your mitochondria

### erixsparhawk — 2013-07-24T23:22:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-gaining-weight-back-at-4-months.5110/post-90752>

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=88823)
>
> That is the difficult part ... to teach your body to use fat for fuel. I think that is my problem. My triglycerides have been consistently increasing on ketogenic and high fat diet. When I was low carb, moderate carbs, my trigs were 55, now they are 261.

I've been continuously in ketosis for over a year now and have done extensive blood testing. Here is what fixed it for me: Your trigs will reduce by about 25% if you cut out dairy and will drop back down to original levels if you also make a concerted effort to eliminated all sources of omega 6 fats i.e. pork and nuts become a rare indulgence and no more cooking or using fruit and vegetable oil (use tallow, butter, coconut or macadamia oils not olive, avocado, almond, lard, etc.) Lastly if you can't get grass fed meats switch to lean meats for protein and get your fat from other sources.

Essentially you are eating huge quantities of fat on a keto diet and when you calculate the total amount of grams omega 6 consumed you will see it becomes very high. Especially from nut flours.

I'm a firm believer that omega 6 reduction is far more important than supplementing omega 3 to reach balance. There is only so much PUFA that your metabolic pathways can handle and all PUFA is delicate and can easily be oxidized and do damage to your body.

My .02

Â 

Even if I can find GF bones, I still might go with non GF just for the price savings. For eating the meat, I'd stick with GF, but if it's just going to be for broth (well there might be some bits of meat left on the bones to add a "bonus" to the broth), I figure why not save some money if I can.

Â 

Also, is it okay after the broth is made, to take a smaller pot, add some diced onions, celery, carrots (not too many of course to increase the carb content, and make a "soup" or stew out of some of the broth, and have that as a meal?Â  Just for a change from a regular plate type meal.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Â  It's a rogue repairman running lose inside you fixing damaged DNA.Â 

Â 

Research on the use of Hemp Oil found itÂ to be a major factor in DNA repair. Hemp has the perfect ratio of Omega fatty acids (Omega 3 and Omega 6) needed by the human body. One â€˜jobâ€™ of Omega 3 is cellular repair. Hemp is 65% protein, 35% of which is globulin edestin protein. It is the protein that is closest to human globulin, and is very easily digestible by the body.

Â 

The protein is a major factor in DNA repair, as the cells use that protein to correct the DNA damage.

Â 

Because hemp is the highest source of Edestin proteinÂ it'sÂ responsible for boosting the immune system among many of it's benefits. Hemp oil has been proven to lower bad cholesterol, raise HDL, prevent strokes, and more....Â  _see below (1)

Â 

Edestin is a globular protein (biologically active) as opposed to fibrous protein (structural). Edestin is a biologically active protein (most similar to globulins), and biologically active proteins are metabolized and used by the human body to manufacture antibodies (immunoglobulins), hormones, haemoglobin and enzymes.

Â 

Edestin has the unique ability to stimulate the manufacturing process of antibodies against invasive agents and contains a low aggravatory phosphor content (kidney ailments, etc.)

Â 

Globular proteins found in Edestin (and in Alpha 1 globulins, Alpha 2 globulins, Beta globulins and Gamma globulins) are long peptide-chains, precursors for biological proteins essential for life.

Â 

Purdue University; Hemp: A New Crop with New Uses for North America; Ernest Small, David Marcus; 2002_
Â 

**Hemp Oil: Benefits

-   **Hemp oil helps with hormonal balance. Hemp oil is the onlyÂ  edible seed that contains gamma-linolenic acid. G.L.A is the precursor for theÂ  production of the protective and calming prostaglandin PGE1 which helps regulateÂ  hormonal balance and support menopausal health.
    \[\*\]**Hemp oil helps regenerate skinâ€™s protective layer. ThanksÂ  to its content of Omega 3 and Omega 6 fatty acids, hemp oil has a compositionÂ  similar to skin lipids which makes it an excellent natural emollient andÂ  moisturizer. It is an ingredient in many skin and body care products. Hemp oilÂ  helps to calm irritated skin.
    \[\*\]**Hemp oil energizes the skin**. Moisturizing, regenerating andÂ  revitalizing, hemp oil is especially useful for dry, tired or dehydrated skin.Â  Skin regeneration and hydration of dry mature skin are enhanced through the useÂ  of hemp oil. It increases the skin elasticity and water retention capacity inÂ  tissues.
    Hemp oil has the ideal combination of fatty acids**. Hemp oilÂ  is the only vegetable oil that contains Omega 3 and Omega 6 fatty acids in theÂ  ideal ratio optimal for body needs.
    **

**
Â 

Hemp Oil: Remedies**

High Cholesterol Levels**. The essential fatty acids fromÂ  hemp seeds and oil can help lower cholesterol levels by accelerating metabolicÂ  processes. With a faster metabolism, fats burn faster and less are deposited onÂ  top of muscle mass or in artery walls.
Diabetes**. For diabetics, the blood sugar index of hemp oilÂ  is considered low because it has low carbohydrate content. It is also full ofÂ  nutrients that help moderate blood sugar levels.
\[\*\]**Atopic dermatitis. Research has discovered that psoriasisÂ  is caused by a deficiency of omega 6 fatty acids in the body. Therefore, hempÂ  oil is recommended as a complementary treatment. The fatty acids present in hempÂ  oil help improve skin oxygenation and hydration.
\[\*\]**Weakened Immune system. Research shows that the omega-3 andÂ  omega-6 fatty acids improve the immune system and regulate intestinal flora,Â  thus building a natural barrier against microbes and increasing the resilienceÂ  of the body.
Demyelination of Nervous system**. The essential fatty acidsÂ  are necessary for a healthy cell membrane structure, especially the nerve cellsÂ  of the myelin shield (which groups the nerve cells). Hemp oil is recommended forÂ  those who have already suffered from the process of demyelination where nerveÂ  membranes lose their myelin sheath.
Varicose veins**. Like other compounds high in omega-3s, hempÂ  oil can thin your blood, reducing blood clots and varicose veins.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-10T12:57:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-wonders-of-hemp-oil.5112/post-67009>

DUDE ... you're a chef?! Â How'd you go from biology to cooking? Â Or am I remember that wrong?

Cococrack ... mmmm and perfectly legal, LOL. Â I have since improved on the general recipe (and not the first one either) ... take a jar of coconut butter/manna. Â If you're in a cold area you'll need to nuke it about 30 seconds to warm it up enough to move. Â Dump into a bowl with 40 grams -- more or less depending on your taste -- of dark chocolate (the less sugar the better). Â I like to add about 1/4 cup of semi-sweet chocolate chips because it seems to smooth it out a touch but if that's too much sugar you can just skip it. Â Add as much sweetener of your choice as you want. Â I have pure sucralose in my kitchen and it doesn't take much. Last time I used the equivalent of half a cup of sugar and it was just about perfect.

Nuke again for about 30 seconds and then stir stir stir. Â You may need to nuke again if you have a weeny microwave like we do.

Once it's all nice and melted and combined you can either leave it in the bowl and just eat with a spoon when the mood hits or stir in whatever. Â It does take a while to set back up though. Â I put it out on the floor of the porch (ceramic tiles get really cold in the winter so it's like having a walk-in cooler. Â 

Last time I added chopped walnuts and poured it out into a buttered pan (never can have enough butter, right?). Â I'm still working on it. Â I just hack off a piece whenever the mood hits. Â Next time I'm adding hemp seeds. And maybe a few chopped walnuts.

Oh and the original recipe called for pure coconut oil. Â People on another forum could NOT stop eating the stuff hence the name, cococrack. Â I could never get ANY sweetener to evenly distribute through the oil no matter what I tried. Â The butter/manna is a much better medium for that. Â You know how oil doesn't like to mix with anybody.

I have an empty carton of Organic _Manitoba Harvest Hemp Yeah Protein Powder_ sitting on my desk to reorder so wanted to know what the forum thought of Hemp Protein... saw that Jack Kruse ain't a fan of Hemp... **so wondering if there is a protein powder that is acceptable?** I saw about Whey option... but wondering which type and brands. Pumpkin, hemp, squash, pea... all processed to make into a power as I do not have any options for making a healthy green or protein powder. A few years ago I got into a bad habit of having a smoothie 1/4 to whole banana, 1/4 to half a cup low sugar berries (no strawberry or blueberry) different but mostly single protein and green powders with almond or pea milk and the kitchen sink of other stuff from sunflower Lecithin for phosphatidylcholine to nuts,seeds and oils etc.
Me thinks better to just eat organic, 'whole, real, local, seasonal food. I've also noticed that Dr. Kruse is not a fan of flax seed either... I crave toasted flax seed but rarely indulge in due to my hypothyroidism.

### Bob Stirling — 2019-12-02T02:27:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-wonders-of-hemp-oil.5112/post-282973>

[http://www.healing-source.com/](http://www.healing-source.com/) in Alberta, Canada has various hemp products including the protein powder. I have ordered from them and the quality is good. (grown in S. Alberta)

### JanSz — 2019-12-02T02:38:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-wonders-of-hemp-oil.5112/post-282974>

> [Martha Barger said:](/goto/post?id=275009)
>
> I have an empty carton of Organic _Manitoba Harvest Hemp Yeah Protein Powder_ sitting on my desk to reorder so wanted to know what the forum thought of Hemp Protein... saw that Jack Kruse ain't a fan of Hemp... **so wondering if there is a protein powder that is acceptable?** I saw about Whey option... but wondering which type and brands. Pumpkin, hemp, squash, pea... all processed to make into a power as I do not have any options for making a healthy green or protein powder. A few years ago I got into a bad habit of having a smoothie 1/4 to whole banana, 1/4 to half a cup low sugar berries (no strawberry or blueberry) different but mostly single protein and green powders with almond or pea milk and the kitchen sink of other stuff from sunflower Lecithin for phosphatidylcholine to nuts,seeds and oils etc.
> Me thinks better to just eat organic, 'whole, real, local, seasonal food. I've also noticed that Dr. Kruse is not a fan of flax seed either... I crave toasted flax seed but rarely indulge in due to my hypothyroidism.

phosphatidylcholine
\----------------------------
Often ordinary lecitin is called phosphatidylcholine (and that may follow with pointing to a very low price for it).
Ed Kane's blood pressure raises and he explains the similarities and differences.
\------

I have been meaning to ask for ages! Does anyone else get this? Â I getÂ an icyÂ sensation in the solar plexus region (I think I've got the location pinned down right) within a minute or two of exiting my cold bath. I only get it if the water is about 50 F or under. It lasts for just a few minutes and it's quite distinctive....like nothing else I've experienced.

Anyone else get this, or care to speculate/shed some light?

# Thread: mangled finger and wound care
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Priscilla — 2012-12-10T20:40:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67063>

OK, I need some advice. Â I just got home from the ER where I got 10 stitches in my finger, which got mangled with my immersion blender after making avocado oil mayo. Â Yes, very stupid.Â 

This was my first ER visit ever and I'm kind of paranoid about infections etc. Â And the waiting room was filled with people who were coughing and sneezing.

Any recommendations about supplements I should be making sure I take? Â I will only eat seafood and seaweed and bone broth these next few days. Â Anything else? Â  I can't get it wet for 36 hours.

It's interesting that I could feel my body responding to the shock of all of this. Â I don't know how to describe it, but I could feel my body react. Â And when I got there, my BP was 177 over something (I've had it checked twice within the last month and it was 120/80). Â But it went down 20 points when they checked it again.

I'd love to hear your advice.

Wow, it took me forever to type this!Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### nonchalant — 2012-12-10T21:10:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67069>

Oh Priscilla, I'm so sorry to hear that!Â  Ouch!

My first thought for prevention of infection was CT.

As for supplements, perhaps some vitamin C and/or zinc?Â  Topical iodine for the hand.

Take care of yourself.Â  Darn, I didn't know making mayo was a hazardous endeavor.Â  /wp-includes/images/smilies/icon\_wink.gif

### Thor — 2012-12-10T21:15:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67070>

.

First - follow all advice from the ER...Â Â  May need to see your Primary Care for follow up?Â Â  If you see any red "streaks" from the finger that run up your arm - call 911 !Â  Any signs of infection call your doctor.Â  Your condition gets worse - call your doctor.

Â 

Any good Antibiotic cream would be of help - keep it dry and change the bandge as instructions would reflect.Â  Supplements?Â  Hefty dose of garlic, grapefruit seed extract, Beta-Glugans, mega dose of probioticsÂ and perhaps toss in some EPICOR\[sup\]TMÂ Â Â  \[/sup\]

Â 

The old cowboys of yester year used good ol' whiskeyÂ 

Â 

Next - toss out blender and use a large hammer on it lol !Â Â  gurrrr bad blender...

### diane — 2012-12-10T21:56:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67077>

Wow - sorry to hear that Priscilla! Not sure what would help, but just wanted to say hope you heal quickly! You did much better than me in the ER - I usually pass out (or come close). LOL.Â 

Â 

Good luck!

### endless — 2012-12-10T23:10:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67089>

I did this two weeks ago...6 stitches in the pad of my thumb! I didn't change up my supplement regime at all, but I did use colloidal silver spray on the wound after the first 24 hours. Personally I wouldn't use a cream...too moist. I healed up quickly just with clean dry dressings, airing out daily and spraying with silver during my breaks from bandages.

Hope you heal up quickly!

### Jonathin — 2012-12-11T12:41:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67116>

A quality honey applied topically will keep the skin soft and stop or reverse any infection. This is cheap and really works with any skin injury including burns. Yes, you need to put some gauze over it and the honey will thin out over time. Too much is messy and not necessary. In wounds with dirt or particles, honey will clean the wound nicely which is better than the typical cleaning procedure. By the way the use of aloe on deep sutured wounds can heal the surface too quickly before the deeper flesh has knit together.

### nonchalant — 2012-12-11T16:00:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67144>

> **endless said**
> I did this two weeks ago...6 stitches in the pad of my thumb!
>
> Â 

Endless?Â  Another victim of mayo-revenge??Â

### Priscilla — 2012-12-11T17:47:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67150>

Thanks so much for all your suggestions. Â Hmmm, colloidal silver, honey, CT, whiskey, iodine, zinc and vit C. Sounds like a great regimen.Â 

I'm supposed to leave on their dressing until tomorrow, not getting it wet at all. I will air it out for a while when I take it off, I'll use gauze dressings as it all heals. Â I'm not feeling any pain, so that's good. I will keep a good eye on it because I don't want it to get infected.

Thor, I won't be using a hammer on the blender because it was user error/stupidity. I've beat myself up a lot over this already.Â 

Live and learn! Â Thanks, everyone!

### endless — 2012-12-11T20:46:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67161>

> Endless? Another victim of mayo-revenge??

Lol! Not exactly...I was rushing while opening a can and the sharp edge got me.

# Thread: Just had a meal that raised my blood glucose level a WHOPPING 1.7 points!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-a-meal-that-raised-my-blood-glucose-level-a-whopping-1-7-points.5119/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-10T20:42:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-a-meal-that-raised-my-blood-glucose-level-a-whopping-1-7-points.5119/post-67064>

I was out doing some shopping, and came home and had some lunch.

I had a can of crab meat, 6 slices of a small, long, English, Cucumber, 3 mushrooms sliced up, 5 strips of bacon from a butcher shop (OMG so much better than "brand" name from the store), and 8 pistachios. (I know, I know, but hey I had a craving, and it was with my meal.)

Â 

Anyway, before I ate, my blood glucose reading was 4.2 or 75.7 in U.S. units.Â  Two hours after, my blood glucose went up a WHOPPING 1.7 points to 4.3 or 77.4 in U.S. units. That was without taking any diabetes medication.

Â 

Damn, looks like Epi-Paleo can even help lower, or keep blood sugar levels down!

### kathylu — 2012-12-12T11:03:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23-and-me-sale.5124/post-67210>

One of the reasons I did it was because there is no future cost or subscription. However, they already have your genome sequence so as new info becomes available on snp's, diseases, syndromes, etc. you will be able to see your results for those too! I think it's a great bargain. It's also fun...lots of ancestry info, etc. Jack will look at the results too. He often picks up things you might not notice.

Â 

It didn't tell me much that I hadn't figured out from my medical history (except that I have the hemochromatosis mutation), I have an increased risk for heart/kidney disease as compared to the general population. As I said, no surprise...everyone in my family seems to kick off from some heart related ailment. However, it's pretty motivating when you see risk profiles in black and white.

Â

### saben555@gmail.com — 2012-12-11T21:50:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saras-journal.5128/post-67165>

Hi everyone,

I had my first consult with Jack last Wednesday. He says I'm a textbook case for Hashi's, which is surprising since I've mostly been a mystery to previous doctors. I'm getting started with supplements, making dietary adjustments, have a session for a floatation tank, am looking for a local doctor and more. I'll write with more detail.

I took magnesium malate last night for the first time and slept better than I can remember and woke up without a headache and without neck and shoulder pain. I also took my usual supports for sleep-cortisol manager and gaba calm last night. Tonight I will only take the magnesium and see if I have the same result. It was an amazing shift!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-12T09:30:10+0200

### nonchalant — 2012-12-12T10:34:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-primal-spirit.5129/post-67201>

This part really resonates with me:

> **Day 9 Wish:** _Dedicate your free time to things you have become passionate about, and watch your life expand_. Passion does not stem exclusively from one area; it can be different for each of us. For some it might be our job, our hobby, or found in our friends or family. One thing I wish for you today is to learn how to know when you have hit upon your passion. The first things you learn is that you will feel your days are too short. Doing will become your primary task in life, and result will be a secondary goal. You will feel a greater detachment and develop a sense of freedom. You will find happiness in doing precisely what you are doing at this moment all the time.

# Thread: Mountain Cedar....thy art my enemy I shall smite thee!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2012-12-12T01:39:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67181>

Anyway...I was told today pretty much the only thing in the air is Mountain Cedar...and apparently I have allergies.Â  I kept telling my mom it was a cold...until I went to Waxahachie from Lewisville where I live...which is around 1:30 away...or so and my symptoms vanished.Â  So...I was wrong it was allergies.

Â 

I was hoping to get some good tips on getting rid of this allergy and dealing with it until I do.Â  If I understand correctly many types of allergies are "curable", but I'm guessing I'll need to do something specific and it may take time.Â  So I need a good way to deal with symptoms until I get rid of it.Â 

Â 

Thanks for any and all help.Â  This allergy thing sucks...one good thing about Midland, TX...dry not many allergens.Â  Much more in this area and I hope this is one of a very small amount of things I have an allergy to...as its very annoying.

### endless — 2012-12-12T03:13:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67183>

Pycnogenol, nettle tea or extract, beta glucans, and a good omega 3/6 ratio are all things I've heard suggested for seasonal allergies. I hope you can get relief soon!

### fitness@home — 2012-12-12T06:35:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67185>

I live in Houston. Every springtime there is a period where tree pollen is very heavy (it even coats my car with a green layer!). What works for me is taking Quercetin with Bromelain. I use NOW brand.

### Destiny — 2012-12-12T07:04:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67187>

My husband uses cold showers, twice a day. Used to take Claritin-d and Afrin for years. Now, he doesn't have to.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-12-12T10:46:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67204>

there ya go.........

### nonchalant — 2012-12-12T12:17:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67214>

I used to suffer horribly with allergies, mountain cedar and ragweed were the worst, then other tree pollens in spring, and grass pollens.Â  Once I reduced my overall inflammation level, all I get now is a very slightly runny nose on occasion, and sometimes I sneeze.Â  No big deal.Â  No asthma any more, no ear infections, no sinus headaches, no eye infections.

I think if your inflammation level is high, seasonal allergies add quite a bit to that level of inflammation, and your body goes nuts.Â  If your inflammation level is low, you have a good buffer for these type allergies, and you get minimal symptoms.

Â 

That's my story and I'm stickin' to it.Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-12T12:58:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67220>

Interesting, given my last hs-CRP was **0.05** and my allergies went completely out of control last summer and are still bothering me. Â I've been doing cold showers for nearly a year and am finally racking up the CT hours on a regular basis. Â So something else is evidently at work here.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-12T13:23:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mountain-cedar-thy-art-my-enemy-i-shall-smite-thee.5130/post-67226>

If its green and grows I'm allergic. Â if its small fluffy, and meows - I'm deathly allergic (full blown asthma attack that requires hospitalization) Â If it can mold - I'm allergic. Â If it feeds of dust (dust mites/cockroaches) Â I'm allergic.Â 

The only place I don't have allergies (w/the exception to cats) is in CO, UT, ID, MT, WY, Mountains of AZ, NM, and CA. Â  I need high desert step and HIGH elevation to be allergy free.Â 

Â 

other than moving - which we can't do right this instant (But DH is now on board w/in the next couple of years) I've started doing allergy shots. Â in the 3 weeks since I've started I've already noticed that I use my inhaler less. Â the allergist I went to is an immunologist - he pulled labs and did a spirometry - pre/post allergy test - and noted that I don't respond to inhaled steroids. Â he also told me that the oral sigulair wouldn't work for me for preventing an attack, and that eventually after enough allergy shots I can come off that medication. Â he thinks eventually that I'll no longer need the abuterol as well.Â 

Â 

I'm also taking the now brands quercertin - 3x/day. Â definitely helps.

7.Â  _**Critical point: The best time to work out biologically occurs when it is **least likely****_ **to be convenient for you because of our neolithic lives wont allow it.Â  I re-tooled my entire schedule as a surgeon to make this work optimally for me to lose weight and change my body.Â  It is that important biologically to get to optimal.Â  1-5 PM is the ideal workout window**.Â  For best results, try to do the exercise in bright sunlight.

.

I keep beating my drum saying MTHFR 677 & 1298 -Â SNP is extremely serious - yet Jack's stance is it's the exception not the rule.Â  OH?Â  Here's some early proof that will knock your socks off!!

Â 

Solar Cycles & L-MTHF are related to embryo loss.Â  Circadian rhythm can affect an embryo - even before the fetus stage of life.Â  Even before a baby is exposed to light and dark outside of the womb.Â Â  And - the real kicker of this amazing research is a new field of disorders never seen before.Â  They are being called:Â  'solar activity/seasonality modulated developmental disorders' - WOW....Â Â  talk about evolution meets genetic science.Â Â Â  I could get into far more "geek" mode but I'll leave it short for now.

Â 

**solar cycle predicts folate-related human embryo loss:**

# Thread: What can I use to absorb, or sop up all the fat/juice left on my plate?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-i-use-to-absorb-or-sop-up-all-the-fat-juice-left-on-my-plate.5136/>

I'm trying to eat as much fat as I can with my meals as per the Leptin Rx instructions, and can tolerate "drinking" a tablespoon worth after my meal from what's left on my plate...or maybe even a little more. I've also managed to choke down a teaspoon or so of coconut oil from the jar, but at times I still have quite a bit remaining on my plate, and if I put it into a container for later, then I likely haven't consumed enough during my meal to reach the high percentage amount recommended for the meal.

Â 

So in lieu of a nice, soft, warm, bun, or roll, and leafy vegetables, or asparagus etc only absorb so much, what can I use to to sop it up?

Â 

Especially if I'm having fish which contains like 2 or 3 grams of fat only per the macronutrient breakdown.

Â 

Any advice or suggestions would be most appreciated.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-12T18:23:33+0200

### furface — 2012-12-12T18:44:11+0200

But at times it's just a lot of bacon grease for instance, and if I've just finished eating 8 slices or so of bacon as part of my BAB, then I'm kind of baconed out, but still want to consume the fat so I get the proper balance of macronutrients into each meal that I'm supposed to.

Â 

But maybe I'll just close my eyes, down the hatch with it, then run to the john.

### AndrÃ©s — 2012-12-14T05:49:06+0200

I actually pour the grease of my bacon (microwaved) over the two eggs omelette with butter and blue cheese (microwaved) at breakfast.

Â 

I have to confess that I still have a little white potato (always smaller than 100g, I think) with my dinners (broiled/steamed seafood and vegetables) so I can smash it at the end and soak it with all of the olive oil I can. I know, I know

... I haven't went all the way to epi-paleo low carb on winter yet.

### Charlotte — 2012-12-28T15:05:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-own-christmas-present-will-be-a-special-hat.5137/post-68605>

OMG Inger, I already started to laugh, when I read the first post, as my imagination was running wild. But when I saw your hat I totally cracked up. I almost fell off my couch, but lost my laptop and lost the threat, so I had to log in again. I am still laughing and you know what, I imagined you in a knights armour with a visir to put up and down and when I saw the picture, it was just as I had imagined. And the idea of you sweating under it. Gosh, you made my day.

Why don't you just convince your employer, to make his restaurant into a middle ages restaurant, where people can eat with their hands, get huge roasts with bone and all and you can wear a knights armour waiting on people?

This is so good. I needed a good laugh tonight and here it is. I am probably still laughing when I go to bed. You didn't just make my day, you made my dreams as well.

How to make candles? In my hippie days I used to make candles to make some money selling them on the flea markets. Its easy. Melt the wax and pour it into a pan, maybe 1/4 inch or more thick. Wait for it to cool down, so it is still manageable. Then take a wick, place it along one side and start rolling the wax into a tube. I used to use the baking pan as it has one side with a straight end and the other a 45Â° angle. Roll the straight side last. Then you have a nice candle. If you like you can sprinkle some color into the hot wax. It makes some nice effects. To buy parafin and stearin is cheap, then you can make your own candles in all sizes. Bees wax is nice too, but hard to get and expensive.

# Thread: Do I HAVE TO eat 3 meals per day when starting out on the Leptin Rx?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-12T16:24:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67249>

Just wondering if it's mandatory to eat 3 meals per day when starting out on the Leptin Rx?

Some days I feel like having 3 meals, others, I only feel like having 2. Or maybe 2 with a light "snack" in lieu of lunch in between. (As long as that "snack" consists of Epi-Paleo approved food. i.e. a piece of dark chocolate, a few pieces of shrimp etc, but not a "full" meal. Or is it vital to get as much fat ingested at the start of Leptin Rx to start the whole adaptation/weaning off of carbs process, and to initiate fat burning, or the Leptin sensitivity activation?

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-12T18:17:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67264>

You want to eat BAB upon rising like it's your religion. After that the guidelines I believe are no snacking between meals which should be 4 hours apart with your last meal 4 hrs before bed. When you start eating a proper BAB(lots of fat and protein) you should not be hungry for lunch. After BAB you should do what feels right. If you're not hungry, don't eat.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-12T18:22:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67265>

Yup, everything Dali said.....

Â 

I started out eating three meals for several months.Â  Then I went to two, back to three, and now I am steady at two meals a day.Â  I've been at this for almost a year.Â  But most importantly is the BAB.Â  Everyone is different and will need/want differently.Â  Find your sweet spot and your food life will change for the better...you will just "know".

### caroline — 2012-12-12T19:15:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67270>

after all this time - I am wondering if I am doing this right.
I read - when you become LS you can cut back on the BAB. For some reason - I don't remember why - I changed my eating patterns. I started eating only when I was hungry. It just seemed intuitive.
I would eat pretty lightly around 10am. A couple of BPC and some protein - depending on my hunger signals.
Then - dinner - around 4:00pm or even earlier if I was hungry and I ate until I was full - sometimes a lot and sometimes not much. I feel terrific.
Now - I am very hungry when I wake up - so I eat then. Dinner is still around/between 2:30 and 4:00pm.
I am assuming that since I am following my bodies cues that this is okay? previously by body wouldn't have had a clue about signalling if it had risen up and hit it in the face!

### furface — 2012-12-12T19:36:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67272>

Oh I have no problem with the BAB, I'm following those "orders" faithfully, and religiously.

I was just wondering if when starting out on the LR, and esp being overweight if 3 meals per day, esp so that we can ingest as much fat as we can per day to get our bodies adpated to eating no grains, no legumes, no dairy per se, limited fruits etc, and to get our bodies Leptin sensitive again is basically mandatory.

I've had no cravings for snacks between meals...surprisingly, or even at night.

It's just that at times after my BAB I'm not really all that hungry until dinner time around 6 P.M., but I don't want to throw my Leptin Reset out of whack if I don't ingest enough proteins or fats throughout the day.

I honestly never thought I'd be posting a topic on wanting to eat less.

I just want to do this right, and as best as I can.

### caroline — 2012-12-13T01:09:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-have-to-eat-3-meals-per-day-when-starting-out-on-the-leptin-rx.5138/post-67311>
